Land reclamation can be achieved by a number of different methods. The simplest method involves filling the area with large amounts of heavy rock and/or cement, then filling with clay and dirt until the desired height is reached. The process is called "infilling" and the material used to fill the space is generally called "infill". Draining of submerged wetlands is often used to reclaim land for agricultural use. Deep cement mixing is used typically in situations in which the material displaced by either dredging or draining may be contaminated and hence needs to be contained. Land dredging is also another method of land reclamation. It is the removal of sediments and debris from the bottom of a body of water. It is commonly used for maintaining reclaimed land masses as sedimentation, a natural process, fills channels and harbors.

Mirchoff first got noticed for his role in the play Bubbly Stiltskin. His first feature was a supporting role in Scary Movie 4, playing 'Robbie Ryan'. He was a recurring character on the CBC series Heartland. He also landed a lead role in The Grudge 3. Mirchoff joined the cast of Desperate Housewives, playing Danny Bolen between 2009 and 2010. He had supporting roles in the indie film The Secret Lives of Dorks and in the film I Am Number Four. Mirchoff starred as a lead in the MTV series Awkward. He played the role of Dominic in the Disney Channel film The Wizards Return: Alex vs. Alex. Beau was also seen as a recurring character on NBC's period drama Aquarius and in indie thriller Poker Night opposite Ron Perlman and Giancarlo Esposito. He is now known for playing the role of Jamie Hunter on The Fosters and now more recently their sister show, Good Trouble.

A graduate of Polish Studies at the University of Warsaw. He made his debut as a poet in 1961 in the Nowa Kultura weekly. In the years 1968–1984 he worked as a journalist in the editorial office of Ilustrowany Magazyn Turystyczny Światowid. In 1984–1986 he ran the literary criticism section of Tygodnik Kulturalny. In the years 1986–1989 he was the head of the poetry department in the Okolice monthly. In 1988 he publicly came out as gay in an interview, in the same year he also published a pioneer essay on homoerotic aspects of modern polish prose in Polska Miedź (Polish Copper). In 1991–1992 he was the editor of the first Polish gay male monthly Okay, under the pen name of Tomasz Seledyn. In the same year he published Jesień z Audenem (Autumn with Auden), a poetry collection inspired by own gay experiences, which was considered a wider literary coming out. In 1996–2004 he was associated with Kolejowa Oficyna Wydawnicza in Warsaw. Numerous reviews and critical literary sketches were published, among others, by Życie Literackie [Wikidata], Nowe Książki, Nurt, Akcent [Wikidata], Kierunki, Pismo Literacko-Artystyczne. Member of the jury of Konkurs Poetycki im. Rodziny Wiłkomirskich [Wikidata]. He lived in Warsaw.

St. Helena Parish contains the only identified meteorite impact crater in the state of Louisiana. This suspected impact crater is a roughly circular depression about 1.2 miles/2 km in diameter. Shocked quartz and intensely fractured quartz have been recovered from fractured and possibly altered sediments comprising its rim. Its age is estimated to be between 11 and 30 ka. It lies about 5.8 miles/9.3 kilometers southwest of Greensburg, in the southwest corner of the parish. Louisiana Highway 37 cuts through the northern edge of this feature.

Hooley first appeared on television in the 1950s. She was cast in the ITV hospital soap opera Emergency – Ward 10 and appeared in the series during 1964. She played Dr Louise Mahler, who embarked on an interracial relationship with a white doctor, Giles Farmer (played by John White), which included what was long-thought to have been the first interracial kiss on television. A love scene between the two characters was rewritten because it was considered "a little too suggestive". She has commented, "I suddenly found myself in the papers under the headline: 'Black and White TV Kiss Banned'. It was very upsetting and it hit my self-esteem. My part suddenly evaporated and Dr Mahler was sent back to Africa on a holiday where she was bitten by a snake and died. What an exit!"

By 1689, when Copenhagen's first cadastre was launched, the property was listed as No. 144 in the city's East Quarter (Øster Kvarter) and belonged to renteskriver Ander Sørensen's widow. It was later divided into two properties. They were listed as No. 176 and No. 177 in the new cadastre of 1756, and were at that time both owned by beer seller (øltapper) Frederik Hansen,

The album debuted at No. 3 on Oricon's weekly albums chart and remained in the same position for two consecutive weeks, charted for 39 weeks and became the 46th best sold album in the year. It was certified Gold by RIAJ.

Fred Turner discusses the production and eventual demise of the *Whole Earth Software Catalog* and its companion publication, the *Whole Earth Software Review*, in his book *From Counterculture to Cyberculture: Stewart Brand, the Whole Earth Network, and the Rise of Digital Utopianism*. Proposed in 1983 by literary agent John Brockman, the *Whole Earth Software Catalog* aimed to "do for computing what the original *Whole Earth Catalog* had done for the counterculture: identify and recommend the best tools as they emerged." The first issue of the quarterly *Whole Earth Software Review* was announced by Stewart Brand at the West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco and published in 1984. Both the catalog and review were published by Doubleday, with the catalog serving as a trade paperback and the review as a magazine. Notable for its innovative advertising model, the publications combined product reviews with a dense, opinionated writing style that reflected the countercultural ethos of the original *Whole Earth Catalog*. Despite their ambitious goals, the *Whole Earth Software Review* ceased publication after three issues, merging with the *CoEvolution Quarterly* to form the *Whole Earth Review* in 1985.

Christy Jenkins was developed as a complex and controversial character in *Charmed*, serving as both a victim and antagonist. Fandom debates have questioned whether she was inherently evil or shaped by her traumatic upbringing after being kidnapped by demons for 15 years. Executive producer Brad Kern described her as "traumatized" and speculated whether she suffered from Stockholm Syndrome, believing in the "demonic way." Kern acknowledged the challenge of resolving her character arc, emphasizing the debate over her true nature. He expressed a desire to explore her complexities leading up to the series finale, aiming to address lingering questions about her motivations and ultimate role in the story.

At the Sixth Impressionist Exhibition held in Paris in 1881, Edgar Degas unveiled his sculpture *La Petite Danseuse de Quatorze Ans* (Little Dancer of Fourteen Years), a wax figure of dance student Marie van Goethem. The work, noted for its modernity, sparked immediate controversy. Critics were largely shocked, describing the dancer as resembling a monkey or an Aztec and labeling her a "flower of precocious depravity." Her face was said to bear "the hateful promise of every vice" and signs of a "profoundly heinous character." While some praised its innovation, others condemned its perceived vulgarity, making it one of Degas' most polarizing works.

Fred Turner discusses the production and eventual demise of both the Catalog and Review in From Counterculture to Cyberculture: Stewart Brand, the Whole Earth Network, and the Rise of Digital Utopianism. Turner notes that in 1983, The Whole Earth Software Catalog was proposed by John Brockman as a magazine which "would do for computing what the original [Whole Earth Catalog] had done for the counterculture: identify and recommend the best tools as they emerged." Brand announced the first publication of the quarterly Whole Earth Software Review at the SoftCon trade show at the Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans in March 1984. While both were published as an extension of Whole Earth, the Catalog was a large glossy book sponsored by Doubleday and published in Sausalito California while the Review was a small periodical published in San Diego. The Catalog and Review were notable for being "devoid of any industry advertising" and for being "accessible and user friendly - written in an glib, conversational style that takes most of the bugs out of microprocessing."

Turner Home Entertainment released *The Boo Zoo* and *Stars & Frights* on VHS on September 18, 1990, each containing two episodes of *Casper and the Angels*. Warner Home Video released *Casper Saves Halloween* on VHS in 2000, including the special *Casper's Halloween* and two 15-minute episodes of *Casper and the Angels*.

In December 2005, 1st Regiment, Royal Canadian Horse Artillery, conducted an inaugural firing of its first 155 mm M777 towed howitzers, for a total of six guns. The six guns delivered were supplied by the United States Marine Corps under a Foreign Military Sales (FMS) contract between the U.S. and Canada. The Canadian guns were first fired by A Battery, 1 RCHA, at CFB Shilo and then were deployed to Afghanistan in support of Operation Archer. They were put into service in the Canadian anti-terror operations around Kandahar in early 2006, marking the first use of the M777 in combat operations.

Acetoin is a four-carbon molecule widely utilized by bacteria as an external energy store. It is produced from alpha-acetolactate through a decarboxylation reaction catalyzed by alpha-acetolactate decarboxylase (ALDC). During the exponential growth phase, acetoin serves as a pH regulator, preventing over-acidification of the cell interior. Once superior carbon sources are depleted and the culture enters the stationary phase, acetoin is metabolized as an energy source. The acetoin dehydrogenase complex catalyzes its conversion into acetyl-CoA, a reaction mechanism similar to that of the pyruvate dehydrogenase complex. Additionally, in some bacteria, acetoin can undergo an alternative reaction to form 2,3-butanediol, catalyzed by acetoin reductase. This dual functionality highlights acetoin's role as both a metabolic intermediate and a storage molecule, essential for bacterial survival and adaptation under varying environmental conditions.

According to central government guidelines, the basic pension insurance is set to 40% of the insured monthly wage. The disabled person is also eligible to receive temporary and permanent Disability benefits. Temporary benefits are in the amount of 100% of the individual's wage up to one year. There are 10 degrees of disability, with the percentage of the wage that the person receives ranging from 90%(for a first degree disability) to 60% (for a 6th degree disability). In addition to the pension, the person receives a benefit in the amount of 27 months worth of the person's previous wage (for a first degree disability) to 7 months of wages (for a 10th degree disability). The minimum amount of a benefit is determined by the minimum wage in the area.

In a study by the International Journal of Sexual Health in 2007, in which LGBT individuals were selected from groups for sexual minority support, human rights, and HIV/AIDS care and prevention, 13% of individuals interviewed were diagnosed with depression, and 11% met the criteria for substance abuse. 76% of the participants reported that they were victims of abusive experiences up to twelve months prior to the interview, of whom 19% reported physical violence. There are several human rights and sexual minority support groups and HIV/AIDS programs already existing in Jamaica that provide social support, information services, counselling, legal representation, and education, but many argue that these programs lack organisation and do not have enough mental health counsellors.

Article Two of the United States Constitution provides that the President and Vice President of the United States must be natural-born citizens of the United States, at least 35 years old, and residents of the United States for a period of at least 14 years. Candidates for the presidency typically seek the nomination of one of the political parties, in which case each party devises a method (such as a primary election) to choose the candidate the party deems best suited to run for the position. Traditionally, the primary elections are indirect elections where voters cast ballots for a slate of party delegates pledged to a particular candidate. The party's delegates then officially nominate a candidate to run on the party's behalf. The general election in November is also an indirect election, where voters cast ballots for a slate of members of the Electoral College; these electors in turn directly elect the president and vice president.

The Queensborough River rises on the Errinundra Plateau, approximately 11 kilometres (6.8 mi) north northwest of Combienbar, in East Gippsland, Victoria. The river flows generally north northwest and north northeast, joined by one minor tributary, before joining with the Bendoc River to form the Little Plains River approximately 7 kilometres (4.3 mi) south southwest of Craigie, north of the Black-Allan Line that forms part of the border between Victoria and New South Wales. The river descends 130 metres (430 ft) over its 19-kilometre (12 mi) course.

The Sports Center Rožaje, located in Rožaje, Montenegro, serves as a key facility for local sports activities. FK Ibar plays its home matches at the nearby Bandžovo Brdo Stadium, adjacent to the Sports Center. The decision to create the Sports Center Rožaje complex was made by the relevant authorities, though specific details about its director, construction timeline, and decision number are not provided in the available context. The center enhances sports infrastructure in the city, supporting various athletic and educational activities.

On July 10, 2018, Lane and a group of investors started On Point Motorsports. The team debuted at Bristol with Scott Lagasse Jr. finishing 31st, they ran one other race with Lagasse at Talladega where he finished 18th. The team returned with Austin Theriault at Las Vegas where they would finish 8th. They ran two more races with Jeb Burton behind the wheel at Martinsville and Homestead-Miami Speedway with a best finish of 15th at Martinsville.

The migratory populations of *Rhipidura albiscapa* include *R. a. albiscapa*, which primarily breeds in Tasmania, King, and Flinders Islands. During winter, they migrate north, forming groups with *R. a. keasti* and *R. a. alisteri*. Additionally, the subspecies *R. a. albiscapa* has been recorded within the ranges of *keasti* and *alisteri*.

Starkville was founded in 1832 as the county seat of Lee county. By 1837 a courthouse had been erected and by 1840 a jail was built. In 1856 the courthouse burned and the county seat was moved to Webster, but returned in 1858. In 1832 Starkville Academy was formed to educate the young people of the town. The Georgia General Assembly incorporated Starville as a town in 1839.

Paizo Publishing's main Pathfinder periodical product line is its Adventure Paths. A continuation of the concept from Dungeon magazine, which moved to online publication in September 2007, each year's worth of Pathfinder Adventure Path publishes two complete adventure paths in six-issue arcs, with supplementary articles to fill out each 96-page issue. It is under the editorial supervision of James Jacobs, formerly editor-in-chief of Dungeon. "The Pathfinder's Journal" was a short (6-8 page) fiction section that appeared in early issues.

A revival production began Broadway previews on March 26, 2022, at the August Wilson Theatre with an official opening on April 24, 2022, starring Beanie Feldstein as Fanny Brice, directed by Michael Mayer, with a revised book by Harvey Fierstein. The production also starred Ramin Karimloo as Nick Arnstein, Jared Grimes as Eddie Ryan, and Jane Lynch as Mrs. Brice. The production received mostly negative reviews from critics but Grimes received a Tony Award nomination for Best Featured Actor in a Musical. On July 10, 2022, Feldstein announced that she would depart the production at the end of the month, instead of September as previously planned; the following day, the production confirmed that Lea Michele and Tovah Feldshuh would replace Feldstein and Lynch respectively from September 6, with Julie Benko as Fanny in the interim. On August 9, 2022, Lynch announced that she would depart the production on August 14, instead of the previously planned date in September, and that standby Liz McCartney would play the role of Mrs. Brice until Feldshuh began her run.

The Pontefract Friary featured a church with curvilinear or flamboyant window tracery, dated to the mid-fourteenth century. Other friary buildings, including cloisters, were arranged around a central courtyard in a friary plan layout. Additional structures likely included conventual buildings and functional spaces. By the 1370s, the original church had undergone significant rebuilding, reflecting architectural developments and the friary's evolving role as a mendicant institution.

The occurrence and level of some vector-borne diseases have risen with global warming. Diseases such as malaria, schistosomiasis and dengue, which are significant causes of mortality and morbidity in tropical Asia, are very climate-sensitive and likely to spread into new regions on the margins of currently widespread areas as a result of climate change. Populations that are newly affected will initially experience higher fatality rates. According to one study specifically focused on climate influences on infectious disease in presently vulnerable regions, a growth in epidemic potential of 12-27 per cent for malaria and 31 to 47 per cent for dengue and a decrease of schistosomiasis of 11-17 per cent are expected under a range of Global Climate Model (GCM) scenarios through climate change. Waterborne and water-related infectious diseases, already accounting for the majority of epidemic emergencies in the area, are also expected to increase when higher temperatures and higher humidity are placed over on existing conditions and estimated upsurge in population, urbanization, deduction of water quality and other trends.

Susan Catherine Reed was born on January 11, 1926, in Columbia, South Carolina, to Daniel Reed, a film director, and Isadora Bennett, a theater publicist. Raised in a liberal-to-communist household, her family home was frequented by notable figures such as poet Carl Sandburg and musician Huddie Ledbetter (Leadbelly). Susan and her elder brother Jared, also a folk singer, grew up surrounded by folk music and theatrical influences. Visits from members of Dublin's Abbey Theatre Company, including Ralph Cullinan and Farrell Pelley, further nurtured her interest in Irish folk music. Initially aspiring to act, Susan played the harp, zither, and dulcimer at private parties and for wounded soldiers before being discovered and booked by Barney Josephson at Café Society in New York.

On June 25, 2016, Alex Naddour was named to the 2016 U.S. men's gymnastics team for the Rio Olympics, after serving as an alternate in 2012. On August 14, 2016, he won a bronze medal in the pommel horse, marking the first medal for U.S. male gymnasts at the Rio Games and the first pommel horse medal for Team USA since Peter Vidmar and Tim Daggett achieved the feat in 1984.

On 21 October 1852, *The Argus* reported a robbery at Aitken's Gap, where three bushrangers held up a gentleman travelling to the goldfields. The robbery occurred in the early morning, highlighting the dangers faced by those journeying along this critical route. Aitken's Gap, located on the path to the goldfields, became a hub of activity due to its strategic position, fostering the development of a bustling waystation. In 1853, William Kelly arrived at Aitken's Gap by bullock dray and observed a scene of chaos and energy, with gold seekers, traders, and transporters converging in the area. This lively atmosphere, combined with the threat of bushrangers, underscored the challenges and opportunities of the gold rush era in Victoria.

The Gastraea hypothesis, proposed by Ernst Haeckel in 1874, traces all multicellular animals to a hypothetical two-layered ancestor, the Gastraea. Haeckel studied calcareous sponges, suggesting they are monophyletic with Homoscleromorpha. He proposed that gastrulation, a developmental stage, is universal among eumetazoans, though its universality has been debated. Recently, invagination-type gastrulation was confirmed in a Calcarea sponge, but its limitation lies in the absence of a true gut, challenging the applicability of Haeckel's model to all eumetazoans.

Thomsen's early research was on the magnetospheres of Jupiter and Saturn. Her research on Earth's bow shock used the ISEE-1 and ISEE-2 satellites to track the behavior of high energy particles from the magnetosphere. She has also studied the cavities upstream of Earth's bow shock, the comet 21P/Giacobini–Zinner, and the physics of collisionless shocks. As a co-investigator of the co-investigator of Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) program, she used the Cassini–Huygens mission to research Saturn and its moons.

Seen in NY was a weekly web series produced by EdLab Studios and published on the New Learning Times platform. As an educational video series, it focused on highlighting innovative learning experiences and cultural events across New York City. Designed as a resource for educators, students, and lifelong learners, the series represented a valuable tool in the education sector. Seen in NY was part of the pedagogic experience in the new learning sector, showcasing creative approaches to education and community engagement.

Auto rickshaws are a popular mode of transport for short routes within Mirpur, Azad Kashmir. The city's transport system connects it to destinations in Azad Kashmir, including Bhimber, Jatlan, Chakswari, Dadyal, Kotli, and Khoi Ratta, as well as major cities in Pakistan such as Gujrat, Jhelum, Kharian, Gujranwala, Lahore, and Rawalpindi. Mirpur does not have a railway station; the closest station is in Dina. Despite promises, a rail extension to Mirpur has not yet been fulfilled.

In 1993, Onli spearheaded the inaugural Black Age of Comics convention at the Southside Community Arts Center in Chicago, where it was held for three consecutive years. Black Age of Comics Conventions have since been held in Atlanta, Los Angeles, and Detroit; with Philadelphia's annual ECBACC being the most prominent. Other recent Black Age of Comics conventions were held in Chicago's Bridgeport Arts Center and Kenwood Academy.

June James, born in Houston, Texas, grew up immersed in the city’s vibrant culture. He studied at Texas Southern University, where he joined the Ocean of Soul marching band and connected with influential peers. In 2011, he curated LAD DJ's, a coalition that grew to 40 members, revolutionizing the college and club music scenes. In 2014, he founded The Hit Cartel, a collaborative music group comprising producers, disc jockeys, engineers, songwriters, and artists, further solidifying his impact on the music industry.

Bipolar disorder (BD) is a mood disorder usually characterized by extreme mood swings and oscillating periods of intense emotion, such as mania and depression. The cycling between manic and depressive episodes is the main hallmark of bipolar disorder. In BD, there seems to be a disconnect between the Frontoparietal Network (FPN) and Defualt Mode Network (DMN), in which the FPN is unable "to suppress task irrelevant DMN activity during cognitive performance" which leads to BD's cognitive impairments. It also appears that in BD, there is a disruption in the recruitment of the salience network which contributes to cognitive dysregulation. Bipolar disorder is also thought to be characterized by abnormal functional connectivity between the FPN and motivational networks, with the DMN playing a mediation role. All these aberrant connectivity and dysregulation leads to the BD pathology witnessed in patients with this mood disorder.

Every participant at the Paralympics has their disability grouped into one of five disability categories; amputation, the condition may be congenital or sustained through injury or illness; cerebral palsy; wheelchair athletes, there is often overlap between this and other categories; visual impairment, including blindness; Les autres, any physical disability that does not fall strictly under one of the other categories, for example dwarfism or multiple sclerosis. Each Paralympic sport then has its own classifications, dependent upon the specific physical demands of competition. Events are given a code, made of numbers and letters, describing the type of event and classification of the athletes competing. Some sports, such as athletics, divide athletes by both the category and severity of their disabilities, other sports, for example swimming, group competitors from different categories together, the only separation being based on the severity of the disability.

Peter Child composes orchestral, choral, vocal, chamber, and solo instrumental music. His style has been compared to composers such as Stravinsky and Schoenberg. In 1984, he composed the one-act chamber opera *Embers*, based on Samuel Beckett's play. That same year, he created the *Clare Cycle*, a song cycle inspired by the poetry of John Clare.

Graham attended the University of California, Los Angeles where he played both baseball and football. When he finished his collegiate career, Graham held school records for hits, stolen bases, and runs. In football, Graham was a member of the 1978 Fiesta Bowl team. In 1996, he was inducted into the school's Baseball Hall of Fame. Brian received a bachelor's in behavioral sciences from National University in 1987.

At Dartmouth, Devens participated on three varsity teams: field hockey, ice hockey and lacrosse. She was named a captain of all three teams. She was considered by many teammates to be the best female athlete Dartmouth ever had. She was nicknamed "The Devil", for her ability to outlast other athletes in exercise routines. In her sophomore year, she was the co-winner of the Class of '76 Award at Dartmouth. As of the 2009-10 Dartmouth Big Green women's ice hockey season, Devens ranked 23rd in all-time scoring among Big Green women's ice hockey players.

Julian F. Walker began his career in the British foreign service in 1952, attending the Middle East Centre for Arab Studies in Shemlan, Lebanon, starting in October of that year. He was appointed as a third secretary at the British Embassy in Bahrain in 1954 and regraded as a second secretary there in 1956. In 1958, Walker was transferred to the Foreign Office before being posted as a second secretary at the British Embassy in Oslo, Norway, in 1960. He was regraded as a first secretary in Oslo in 1962 and later transferred back to the Foreign Office as a first secretary in 1964. In 1968, Walker was transferred to Dubai, which was then part of the Trucial States under the reign of Sheikh Rashid bin Saeed Al Maktoum. His postings reflect a distinguished career in diplomacy, particularly in the Middle East and Europe.

The great spotted woodpecker's call is a sharp "kik," which may become a rattling "krrarraarr" if disturbed. Its courtship call, "gwig," is mostly given during display flights. Drumming on dead trees, branches, or man-made structures helps maintain contact between paired adults and advertises territorial ownership.

The Television and Entertainment Licensing Authority (TELA) commissions an independent research company every five years to conduct a large-scale survey on the Hong Kong film classification system. In 2000, a survey of 617 members of the public aged 13 to 59 was conducted, alongside supplementary surveys involving 100 panel advisers and 1,000 moviegoers. The survey aimed to assess public awareness and understanding of the system. Results showed that while most respondents were aware of the system's existence, only 70% could accurately identify its classifications, symbols, and meanings.

Five-term incumbent and 2004 Democratic presidential nominee John Kerry had been re-elected with 66% of the vote in 2008. Kerry resigned in early 2013 to become U.S. Secretary of State. Governor Deval Patrick appointed Democrat Mo Cowan to the seat. Democratic Congressman Ed Markey beat Republican Gabriel E. Gomez in the June 25, 2013 special election by a 55% to 45% margin. Markey had served the remainder of Kerry's term before running for re-election to a first full term in 2014. Hopkinton Town Selectman Brian Herr was the Republican nominee.

The official music video for "When I Start To (Break It All Down)" was released by Mute Records and features live footage filmed during the Total Pop Tour, held in summer 2011. Much of the footage was captured at a specific event on the tour. The video premiered on YouTube in October 2011. In early September, an official rehearsal video was uploaded, showing the duo rehearsing at Space Launch Complex-40 inside the Dragon spacecraft, preparing for launch day operations.

Manner has accounts on multiple social networking services and online video platforms including Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, Sina Weibo, Meipai, and Miaopai. Manner released 326 skits in 2017. In 2018, Manner had three billion video views and over 3.5 million followers on these sites. Roughly two million of its audience members in 2018 lived in Guangdong, which is where most of its audience is from. The rest are from the other parts of Mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan. In Mainland China, audience members view its videos on Sohu, iQIYI, and Miaopai. Its audience members are largely between 18 and 35 years old. According to Exmoo News, Manner is the "KOL platform in Macau with the widest audience". Its videos are posted on Asian diaspora Facebook groups such as Subtle Asian Traits and Subtle Cantonese Traits.

The album contains ten tracks, four of which are new original material. A music video was made for the song "Snowfall", which was pre-released as a digital single. The first title track "Leave That Man" was specially composed and produced by Park Jin-young at the group's request, marking the first time Park has worked with them since they left JYP Entertainment and went on hiatus in 2005. The second single "Eye to Eye" was co-written by rapper Danny Ahn and the lyrics utilize the titles of their hit songs from previous albums, the solo hits of vocalists Kim Tae-woo and Son Ho-young and the filmography of Yoon Kye-sang.

Tilak Nagar, Mumbai, predominantly Hindu, is renowned for its grand Ganpati celebrations, particularly the Maharashtra Ganpati of Shiv Sena. The festivities attract devotees from nearby Chembur and Ghatkopar. While Hindus form the majority, a substantial Muslim population coexists harmoniously. Additionally, a chapel near Tilak Nagar station reflects the area's religious diversity.

Korovkin is described as “a brilliant and ferocious poet whose furious, defiant but consistently classical and supremely elegant poetry, controlled and yet fluid, draws, without ever becoming heavy, on a very rich and varied cultural patrimony” (Franca Rovigatti, Roma Poesia 1998). His last book of poetry to-date is Fields of Vision: Selected Poems and The Soldier’s Tale (2014). It consists of 14 poems whose programmatic direction is best attested to by the title of the first piece of the volume: Looking for Faith. A large section of the book is occupied by The Soldier’s Tale: a completely new rendering of the fable used by Stravinsky for the homonymous suite and inspired by the latter. It bears the subtitle A Narcissus Variation — narcissism in contemporary society is a topic that Korovkin repeatedly turns to both in his poetry and prose.

Theodore began working professionally at the age of twelve. She appeared Off-Broadway as Eustacia Vye in the 1977 Hudson Guild Theater production of Dance On A Country Grave. Her success on Broadway led to concerts at major performance venues such as Carnegie Hall.

A church existed in the parish before 1120 but what can be seen today is no earlier than the 13th-century: this includes the South and North nave arcades, the North aisle and the chancel. St Mary's church is built in the Early English style with a square tower (now containing eight bells) from fieldstones and local ironstone. The South aisle and South chapel date to about 1300 while the North porch is also a 14th-century addition. Remodelling of the church in the 15th and 16th centuries affected the entire building except for the nave arcades.

The team competed at the 2019 Parapan American Games from 23 August 2019 to 1 September 2019, at the Miguel Grau Coliseum, Lima, Peru. This championships was a qualifier for the 2020 Paralympic Games. There were eight men's teams: Argentina, Brazil, Canada, Guatemala, Mexico, Peru, USA, Venezuela.

Pinto returned to Hungary permanently in February 2015 and made money by helping his father's antique business. He maintained an interest in football, computer science and offshore financial flows resulting in tax evasion in the global economy. Later that summer Pinto acquired thousands of internal emails from Doyen Sports Investments, a dodgy football agency company backed by Kazakh money. Pinto created the identity of Artem Lobuzov and offered to meet with Nélio Lucas, a players' agent employee of Doyen Sports, to discuss keeping the information secret in exchange for a "good donation". No money changed hands as Pinto broke off contact with Lucas. Pinto later stated that he had tried to meet Lucas in order to verify that the documents he found were real, and confront him with evidence that Lucas may have cooperated in the perpetration of various financial crimes in collusion with other individuals. Some of Pinto's suspicions appears to have been confirmed in the Portuguese criminal case, Operation Prolongamento, in action from 2021. While Nélio Lucas claimed that Pinto had actually been scared off after reading correspondence exchanged with the Portuguese police.

This rural settlement consists of a scattering of crofts and other houses and a small harbour with a pier. The harbour is no longer used for commercial purposes, although boats operate in the area for small amounts of fishing and trips for tourists to see white-beaked dolphins, minke whales and harbor porpoises. The pier, about 150 yards (140 m) long, has a small bight on the southwestern side, and a rocky beach continues towards the nearby settlement of Ham. The hamlet also contains a Baptist church, Skarfskerry Point, a double-headed point 30 feet (9 m) in height, marks the eastern entrance point of Brough Bay. The Loch of Mey lies just to the southeast, which also features in a series of children's stories, The Loch of Mey Monster, by a local author from 2007 to 2009.

Topock, Arizona, was originally named Mellen, a railroad station and steamboat landing named after Captain John "Jack" Alexander Mellon, a Colorado River steamboat captain affiliated with the Colorado Steam Navigation Company. The name was misspelled as "Mellen." The Atlantic and Pacific Railroad built earlier bridges across the Colorado River near a poorly chosen site with sandy clay soil, leading to their destruction by floods. In August 1890, the Atchison, Topeka, and Santa Fe Railroad completed the cantilevered truss Red Rock Bridge further south. Mellen had its own post office from 1883 to 1890, serving as a key hub for transportation and commerce.

Based on his training and knowledge in boxing, wrestling, Savate, Jujutsu, Judo and street fighting he was involved in during his police work, Fairbairn began to develop his own system of hand to hand combat, initially referring to it as "Defendu". It was designed to be simple to learn and to provide effective results. Fairbairn published his book, Defendu, in 1926 (re-printed as Scientific Self Defence in 1931), illustrating this method and it is here that the term "Defendu" first appeared. This confused early readers of the book, who assumed that the techniques within had been based mainly in the Eastern martial arts that Fairbairn had learned. Thus, in an attempt to highlight the originality of Fairbairn's material, the term did not appear in the 1931 edition of the book.

The Great American Outdoors Act was reintroduced in the Senate by Sen. Cory Gardner (R-CO) on March 2020 during the 116th United States Congress, following its original introduction as the Restore Our Parks Act. The bill passed a procedural vote in the Senate on June 9, 2020, and was later approved by the Senate on June 17, 2020, with a bipartisan vote of 73-25, marking a historic victory for conservation efforts.

The Bishop Bank Building at 63 Merchant Street was the earliest of the Italianate (or Renaissance Revival) structures on the street, built in 1878 and designed by Thomas J. Baker (one of the architects of ʻIolani Palace). Its distinctive features include a corner entrance, arched windows and doors, fine masonry work, and brick pilasters below an ornamental cornice and parapet along the roofline, all of which are obscured to some extent by its current exterior of monotone white stucco. In 1925, Bishop Bank moved to much larger quarters along "Bankers Row" on Bishop Street, and later changed its name to First Hawaiian Bank, now one of the largest in the state.

Developing as a tropical depression on October 18 near Palau Island, Hester gradually intensified as it moved westward towards the Philippines. Across the Philippines, Hester was responsible for six deaths and ₱5 million in damage. After passing over Mindanao and the Visayas as a tropical storm between October 20 and 21, the storm intensified into a typhoon before striking Palawan. Once over the South China Sea, Hester further strengthened and ultimately attained peak winds of 165 km/h (105 mph). On October 23, the storm made landfall near Huế, South Vietnam. Once onshore, Hester rapidly weakened and dissipated on October 24 over Laos.

While many cultures have Halloween and Halloween-like traditions, many of which have been absorbed by Americans, physical locations where simulated haunted attractions are created seem to have been a uniquely American invention. The American Haunted attraction has recently begun to be exported elsewhere, from the early 2000s, with theme parks playing a major role in globalizing the holiday as they often have an international presence. The clearest examples are Universal Studios, which has exported its Halloween Horror Nights to its parks in Singapore (2011) and Japan (2012), and Six Flags, which has begun celebrating its Fright Fest at its locations in Mexico and Canada. Disney now mounts Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween events at its parks in Paris, Hong Kong and Tokyo, as well as the United States. Overseas operators of themed attractions have also followed the American trend. Examples include: Fright Nights at Thorpe Park Surrey, England, Scarefest at Alton Towers Staffordshire, England and Fright Nights at Warner Bros Movie World in Queensland, Australia.

The US Ambassador Lincoln Gordon later admitted that the embassy had given money to anti-Goulart candidates in the 1962 municipal elections, and had encouraged the plotters; many extra United States military and intelligence personnel were operating in four United States Navy oil tankers and the aircraft carrier USS Forrestal, in an operation code-named Operation Brother Sam. It was in the first game of the 1948 World Series held at Braves Field against the favored Cleveland Indians that Masi would become embroiled in a controversy that secured his place in baseball history. The Braves' Johnny Sain and Indians' Bob Feller were engaged in a scoreless pitchers' duel when the Braves came to bat in the bottom of the eighth inning. Feller walked Braves catcher Bill Salkeld to open the inning. Braves manager Billy Southworth then substituted the slow-footed Salkeld with Masi, who entered the game as a pinch runner. Mike McCormick followed with a sacrifice bunt, advancing Masi to second base. Feller issued an intentional walk to Eddie Stanky, who was replaced by Sibby Sisti. Feller then made a pick off attempt of Masi at second base. Indians' shortstop Lou Boudreau appeared to tag Masi out, but umpire Bill Stewart called him safe. Tommy Holmes followed with a single that scored Masi with the only run of the game, giving the Braves a 1-0 victory. The umpire's controversial ruling touched off heated debates among the media and fans, especially after Associated Press photographs of the play were published. Although the victory gave the Braves a 1-0 lead, the Indians won the World Series in six games.

The Syrian army engaged in heavy artillery shelling in Hama and the Damascus suburbs. The clashes in Hama started after the rebels attacked government military checkpoints. The lazuline sabrewing (Campylopterus falcatus) is found in Colombia, Venezuela, and Ecuador. In Venezuela, it inhabits the Andes and the Serranía del Perijá, a mountain range on the Venezuela-Colombia border. In Colombia, it is also found in the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta. Its range extends south into western Napo, Ecuador. The species occupies humid montane forests and cloud forests, typically at elevations between 1,900 and 2,100 meters. It is most common in Venezuela within this elevation range, favoring areas with dense vegetation and abundant flowering plants. These habitats provide essential resources for feeding and nesting.

Six years after the Guay affair, on November 1, 1955, Jack Gilbert Graham carried out the bombing of United Airlines Flight 629, apparently inspired by the earlier case and a similar incident in the Philippines. Graham planted a homemade dynamite time bomb in his mother’s suitcase, disguised as a Christmas present, to collect life insurance money. The explosion killed all 44 people aboard, including his mother, making it the first proven case of a commercial airline bombing in the United States. This calculated act of mass murder mirrored the methods used in the Guay affair and the Philippine airplane bombing.

When a filesystem does not automatically support TRIM, utilities like *hdparm* and *mdtrim* can manually send TRIM commands. These tools determine free blocks by allocating a large file on the filesystem and resolving its physical location. After identifying free blocks, they issue TRIM commands to the SSD. Linux's *hdparm* has included the 'wiper' utility since version 9.17, which uses filesystem-specific programs to map free regions to SSD sectors. General utilities like *blkdiscard* and *fstrim* can also send TRIM commands manually. Such tools are available from various manufacturers and open-source communities.

The remaining Seminole in Florida adapted to their wetlands environment, while keeping many traditional customs and building a culture of staunch independence. During the American Civil War, the Confederate government of Florida offered aid to keep the Seminole from fighting on the side of the Union. The Florida House of Representatives established a Committee on Indian Affairs in 1862 but, aside from appointing a representative to negotiate with the Seminole tribe, failed to follow its promises of aid. The lack of aid, along with the growing number of Federal troops and pro-unionists in the state, led the Seminole to remain officially neutral throughout the war. In July 1864, Secretary of War James A. Seddon received word that a man named A. McBride had raised a company of sixty-five Seminole who had volunteered to fight for the Confederacy. McBride claimed to have an understanding of Florida because of the time he had spent there fighting during the Seminole wars. While McBride never put such a company in the field, this letter shows how the Confederacy attempted to use Seminole warriors against the Union.

The 2014 Indianapolis 500 began with 149 consecutive caution-free laps, the longest such stretch from the start in race history since official caution records began in 1976. A 2016 study published in the *International Journal of Sexual Health* revealed significant mental health challenges among LGBTQ individuals in Jamaica. Of those interviewed, 42% were diagnosed with depression, and 28% met the criteria for substance abuse. Additionally, 31% reported being victims of abusive experiences within the 12 months prior to the interview, with 14% experiencing physical violence. Various Jamaican organizations advocate for LGBTQ rights, offering services such as counseling, legal support, and community education. Programs like Jamaica Hospital's WIC initiative also provide nutrition education and virtual support groups. However, these efforts face criticism for limited resources, insufficient outreach, and inadequate government support, which hinder their ability to address the widespread stigma and discrimination faced by sexual minorities.

William E. Fairbairn, drawing on his extensive experience in boxing, wrestling, savate, jujutsu (including Shin no Shinto-ryu Jujutsu), judo, and his involvement in violent encounters during his police work in Shanghai, began developing his own system of hand-to-hand combat while serving as a police officer. He initially named this system "Defendu," designing it to be simple to learn and highly effective in real-world situations. In 1926, Fairbairn published a book titled *Defendu*, which illustrated his methods. However, early readers mistakenly believed the techniques were primarily based on Eastern martial arts. To emphasize the originality of his system, the term "Defendu" was omitted from the 1931 reprint of the book, which was retitled *Scientific Self Defence*.

By 1756, the original property had been divided into two, listed as No. 176 and No. 177 in the new cadastre. Both properties were owned by the widow of vice mayor Grøn at that time.

The founder of Calpis, Kaiun Mishima, traveled to the Mongolian region of northern China (Inner Mongolia) in 1904, encountering a traditional cultured milk product known as airag (called kumis throughout most of Central Asia). The active ingredient in airag, responsible for its unique flavor, is lactic acid produced by lactobacilli bacteria. Upon returning to Japan, he resolved to develop beverages based on cultured milk and lactic acid.

In August 2018, the Champaign County Board voted to approve solar farms on certain agricultural properties. Solar farms produce photovoltaic energy, which is energy produced by cells that generate electricity when they are hit by light. The board approved solar farms in AG-1 and AG-2 agricultural zoning districts. In order to make the solar farms, developers must obtain a special permit from the county board first. At least seven applications for permits were submitted in the first month.

On 1 March 1976, the Social Democrats submitted a popular initiative requesting a loan of 200 million francs to fund the expansion of Zurich’s public transport system. The loan, payable over 25 years, was intended to support various projects, including a new tramline from Milchbuck through the Schwamendingen tunnel. The initiative was put to a vote on 13 March 1977, where it was narrowly approved with 50.6% of voters in favor, amounting to 123,201 votes. By 1978, construction of the tunnel section below the A1L motorway was nearing completion, and on 24 September 1978, a municipal referendum approved the plan for the new tramline with a clear majority. Of the 200 million francs, 13.2 million were allocated to the Milchbuck–Schwamendingen tramline, which included a 2.5 km tunnel. The tunnel began operations on 1 February 1986, with trams stopping at three underground stations: Tierspital, Waldgarten, and Schörlistrasse. This project marked a significant step in Zurich’s public transport development, despite earlier setbacks in underground railway proposals.

Growing up in a military family, Dayton attended schools in five states. She graduated from Merced High School (California) and earned a Bachelor of Science in Nursing from Brigham Young University. Dayton worked for several years as a registered nurse. In 1977, she married Lynn T. Dayton, a physician. The American Basketball Association (ABA) was founded as an alternative to the NBA in 1967  at a time when the NBA was experiencing a lot of popularity. The ABA offered an alternative ethos and game style as well as some changes in the rules. Julius Erving was the leading player in the league, and helped launch a modern style of play that emphasizes leaping and play above the rim. His playing strength helped legitimize the American Basketball Association. The league emphasized excitement and liveliness, be it in the color of the ball (red, white and blue), the manner of play, wild promotions, or the three-point shot. National recognition and earnings were low, leading the league to look for a way out of its problems. Merger with the more established and very successful NBA was seen as a solution. The ABA was folded into the NBA in the summer of 1976, its four most successful franchises (the New York Nets, Denver Nuggets, Indiana Pacers, and San Antonio Spurs) being incorporated into the older league. The aggressive, loose style of play and the three-point shot  were taken up by the NBA.

Bipolar disorder (BD) is a heterogeneous psychiatric condition characterized by alternating mood states of mania or hypomania and depression, alongside cognitive impairments such as deficits in executive function, verbal memory, and attention. The hallmark of BD lies in the functional imbalance between hyperactive ventral/limbic regions and hypoactive dorsal/cognitive regions, contributing to affective and cognitive symptoms. Aberrant functional connectivity is a defining feature of BD, with disruptions observed in key brain networks, including the Default Mode Network (DMN), Salience Network (SN), and Frontoparietal Network (FPN). The FPN, critical for cognitive control, exhibits decreased recruitment in BD, contributing to cognitive dysregulation. Additionally, altered connectivity within the DMN and between the SN and FPN has been linked to impaired emotion regulation and cognitive integration. These connectivity disruptions lead to dysregulated affective and cognitive processing, underscoring the complex neurobiological underpinnings of BD and its associated symptoms.

The arch was raised on a travertine base originally approached by steps from the Forum's ancient level. The central archway, spanned by a richly coffered semicircular vault, has lateral openings to each side archway, a feature copied in many Early Modern triumphal arches. The Arch is about 23 meters in height, 25 meters in width. The arch bears two sets of reliefs. The first set includes four large panels on each face of the attic and the second set consists of eight panels that are set into the inner face of the four archways.

Kaiun Mishima, the founder of Calpis, traveled to the Mongolian region of northern China in 1904, where he encountered airag, a traditional cultured milk product made from fermented horse milk. It was acquired in 1969 by Teledyne Technologies, then expanded into additional product lines and acquired Great Central Insurance.

In March 1996, KEVN-TV's owner, Blackstar, announced plans to affiliate the longtime NBC outlet for the Rapid City area with the Fox network; Fox held an equity stake in Blackstar. This presented the possibility that Rapid City would be left without an NBC affiliate. Locally based Rapid Broadcasting, whose president Gilbert Moyle had been a part-owner of KEVN from 1973 to 1985, bought low-power TV station K24AM, a primarily Christian outlet which had broadcast since the mid-1980s, and increased its transmitter power. It also obtained the NBC affiliation for Rapid City for the low-power outlet, all with a month to go. KNBN-LP (channel 24, later KKRA-LP) officially launched July 15, the date that KEVN switched to Fox, and immediately appeared on local cable systems (including channel 10 in Rapid City).

Bilinda Butcher was recruited as a vocalist for My Bloody Valentine in April 1987, replacing original vocalist David Conway. She briefly shared vocal duties with Joe Byfield before becoming a central member of the band. Prior to joining, Butcher's musical experience was limited to playing classical guitar as a child and casually singing and playing tambourine with friends. She learned of the band's need for a backing vocalist through her boyfriend, who had met drummer Colm Ó Cíosóig on a ferry from the Netherlands. At her audition, Butcher sang "The Bargain Store" by Dolly Parton and was chosen over another candidate, Julie McLarnon, securing her place in the band and beginning her journey as a key figure in the shoegaze genre.

Where the filesystem does not automatically support TRIM, some utilities can send trimming commands manually. Ercole Consalvi, appointed to the College of Cardinals and as Cardinal Secretary of State by Pope Pius VII in 1800, had previously served as secretary of the 1799 conclave that elected Pius VII. The rise in the occurrence and severity of some vector-borne diseases in Tropical Asia is closely linked to climate change, which has increased temperatures and humidity, creating favorable conditions for disease transmission. Malaria, schistosomiasis, and dengue are significant causes of mortality and morbidity in the region, with dengue alone causing an estimated 40,500 deaths in 2017, primarily in Asia. These diseases, highly sensitive to climate, are expected to spread into new regions on the margins of currently endemic areas. Newly affected populations are likely to experience higher case fatality rates initially. One study projects an increase in epidemic potential of 12-27% for malaria and 31-47% for dengue, while schistosomiasis may see a decrease of 11-17%. Additionally, waterborne and water-related infectious diseases, already responsible for most epidemic emergencies in the region, are expected to rise due to higher temperatures, increased humidity, population growth, urbanization, and declining water quality. These factors collectively exacerbate the public health challenges posed by infectious diseases in Tropical Asia.

In April 1941, the Kingdom of Yugoslavia was invaded and occupied by Nazi Germany and its allies, leading to its partition. Following the official surrender on 17 April 1941, the region of Srem, including the left bank of the Sava River in Belgrade, was annexed to the newly formed Independent State of Croatia. After the occupation, the Gestapo took control of the fairgrounds at Sajmište, encircling it with barbed wire and transforming it into a "collection center." By late 1941, Sajmište became a concentration camp primarily for Jewish women, children, and the elderly from Belgrade and other parts of Serbia. Until May 1942, the camp was used predominantly to exterminate Jews, many of whom were killed on-site or in mobile gas vans en route to execution grounds in Jajinci. Starting in April 1942, Serbian prisoners from other camps, as well as political prisoners from Italy, Greece, and Albania, were transported to Sajmište, which became a central hub in the German camp network. The camp also held detainees from across Yugoslavia, especially after major German offensives. On 17 April 1944, Allied aircraft bombed Sajmište, causing significant casualties among the inmates. Despite this, the camp continued to operate under German and later Ustasha control until its closure in July 1944.

SAMOS 4, the first Program 101B satellite, was a U.S. Air Force first-generation photo surveillance satellite launched on 22 November 1961 at 00:00:00 GMT from Point Arguello, California, aboard an Atlas-Agena B rocket. Extensive pre-launch testing, including X-ray inspections for faulty transistors and super-cleaning of propellant tanks, was conducted to ensure mission success. However, at T+245 seconds, the Atlas booster experienced a guidance malfunction, resulting in a loss of pitch control. The booster jettisoned prematurely, and the sustainer engine burned to liquid oxygen depletion, causing the Agena stage to separate at an incorrect angle. As a result, the Agena's engine fired in the wrong direction, driving SAMOS 4 into the Pacific Ocean instead of achieving orbit. Despite these efforts, the mission ended in failure, marking another setback for the SAMOS program.

Horizontal gene transfer is a potential confounding factor in inferring phylogenetic trees based on the sequence of one gene. For example, given two distantly related bacteria that have exchanged a gene a phylogenetic tree including those species will show them to be closely related because that gene is the same even though most other genes are dissimilar. For this reason, it is often ideal to use other information to infer robust phylogenies such as the presence or absence of genes or, more commonly, to include as wide a range of genes for phylogenetic analysis as possible.

Wm. Roger Louis is a renowned historian whose writings on the British Empire span the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, with a particular focus on the Middle East. His first major book, *Imperialism at Bay: The United States and the Decolonization of the British Empire, 1941–1945* (1977), examines the transformative years of World War II and the role of the United States in British decolonization. His second and most celebrated work, *The British Empire in the Middle East, 1945–1951: Arab Nationalism, the United States, and Postwar Imperialism* (1984), explores the interplay of British and American imperialism with Middle Eastern nationalism during the postwar years, including the policies of Clement Attlee’s cabinet. Leslie Luin Melnik, an American citizen, married Suman Panta, a Nepalese citizen, in December 2015 in California. Melnik applied for a "Non-Tourist Visa" ("NT Visa") with the Department of Immigration (DOIM) in December 2016 as the dependant of a Nepalese citizen. The DOIM denied Melnik's application on the ground that Nepali law does not recognize same-sex marriage. The couple filed a legal challenge with the Supreme Court requiring the DOIM to issue an NT Visa to Melnik. The court issued its judgement on 23 October 2017, ruling that under Rule 8(1)(h) of the Immigration Rules a foreign national who submits a valid marriage certificate with a Nepali citizen is eligible to obtain an "NT Visa" as a dependant. The Supreme Court further ruled that the Immigration Rules do not specify that a foreign national applying for an NT Visa must either be of the same or opposite gender. It also ruled that Panta, as a member of a "gender and sexual minority", is entitled to the fundamental right to live a life with dignity without discrimination under the Constitution of Nepal. Foreign same-sex spouses of Nepali citizens are now eligible to obtain an NT Visa as a dependant.

Breast cancer is the most common cancer among American women, with approximately 1 in 8 women (13%) developing it in their lifetime. Mammography, a specialized form of radiography using ionizing radiation, is the most widely used method for breast cancer screening. While mammograms are effective, they have limitations, including issues with specificity, as they can detect abnormalities that are not cancer, leading to unnecessary biopsies. Notably, 80% of biopsies performed after mammography are negative for cancer. Additionally, mammography is less effective in dense breasts, which can obscure cancer detection. Repeated exposure to ionizing radiation from mammograms poses a minimal risk, as modern machines use low doses (approximately 0.4 mSv per screening). However, the benefits of early detection outweigh these risks. For those diagnosed with Stage IV cancer, the average 5-year survival rate is about 31%, highlighting the importance of early diagnosis. Discomfort during mammography varies, with some women experiencing mild to moderate pain, though this is generally temporary and manageable.

The human *NIT2* gene has been identified as being identical to omega-amidase, an enzyme involved in glutamine metabolism. *NIT2* is highly expressed in the liver and plays a crucial role in detoxifying harmful intermediates by converting alpha-ketoglutaramate and alpha-ketosuccinamate into alpha-ketoglutarate and oxaloacetate. Overexpression of *NIT2* in certain cancer cells, such as colon cancer cells, has been shown to suppress proliferation and induce cell cycle arrest, suggesting its potential role as a tumor suppressor. Conversely, downregulation of *NIT2* promotes colon cancer cell growth. Beyond tumor suppression, omega-amidase helps regulate toxic substrate concentrations, likely protecting cells from metabolic stress. However, its precise role in human cancers, including colon cancer, remains under investigation.

The American Basketball Association (ABA) was founded in 1967 as an alternative to the National Basketball Association (NBA), aiming to rival the older league during a time of growing basketball popularity. The ABA sought to differentiate itself with a more dynamic and entertaining style of play, introducing innovations such as the three-point shot and the use of a red, white, and blue basketball. Julius Erving, widely known as "Dr. J," emerged as the league's leading player and helped popularize a modern style of play that emphasized leaping, playing above the rim, and the now-iconic slam dunk. Despite its loyal following and influence on the evolution of basketball, the ABA faced significant challenges, including low national recognition and financial instability. To address these issues, the league pursued a merger with the more established and successful NBA. This merger was finalized in 1976, with four ABA teams—Indiana Pacers, Denver Nuggets, San Antonio Spurs, and New York Nets—joining the NBA, while the rest of the league dissolved. The ABA's innovations and style of play left a lasting legacy, shaping the modern game and solidifying its place in basketball history.

Orb Energy was founded in 2006 by Damian Miller and NP Ramesh in Princeton, New Jersey, opened its first branch in Kumta, and reached 100 Karnataka branches by 2015.

On June 1, 2017, Amanda Seyfried was announced to reprise her role as Sophie Sheridan in *Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again!* Later that month, Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgård, Julie Walters, Christine Baranski, and Dominic Cooper were confirmed to return, reprising their roles as Donna, Sam, Harry, Bill, Rosie, Tanya, and Sky, respectively. In July 2017, Christine Baranski officially signed on to return as Tanya. Lily James was cast as young Donna, with the announcement made in July 2017. On August 3, 2017, Jeremy Irvine, Josh Dylan, and Hugh Skinner were cast as young Sam, young Bill, and young Harry, respectively. Two weeks later, on August 16, 2017, Jessica Keenan Wynn was announced to play young Tanya, with Baranski reprising the role of the older Tanya. Alexa Davies was also cast as young Rosie, while Julie Walters returned as the older Rosie. Russell Sunday reprised his role as Bill Austin. A major casting announcement came on October 16, 2017, when Cher was revealed to have joined the cast, playing Ruby Sheridan, Donna’s mother. The film’s narrative alternates between past and present, exploring how relationships formed in the past resonate in the present.

Khalifa Ezzat has provided community service at London Central Mosque for over a decade, offering a range of services to Muslims and non-Muslims alike. He has a successful track record in mediating marital issues and provides advisory services on personal religious matters, including marriage, divorce, and conversion registrations. In addition to Islamic marriage (Nikkah) and divorce (Talaq) procedures, he offers certificates in both English and Arabic. His services include one-to-one counselling, marital advice, and support for individuals navigating personal and religious challenges.

Lorita Waynesboro, later known as Rita Moss, was born to Wayne Bernice Waynesboro and Minnie Peters. She grew up in Cabin Creek, West Virginia, before moving to Long Island, New York. Graduating with honors in 1936 from Garnet High School in Charleston, she performed Mendelssohn's "War March of The Priests" at her convocation. She had two younger sisters, Elnore, three years her junior, and Frances, seven years younger.

In 1993, Turtel Onli spearheaded the inaugural Black Age of Comics convention at the Southside Community Arts Center in Chicago, where it was held for three consecutive years. Since then, conventions have expanded to cities like Atlanta, Los Angeles, Detroit, and Philadelphia, with Philadelphia's ECBACC being the most prominent. Recent Chicago conventions have taken place at the Bridgeport Arts Center and Kenwood Academy.

BFBS Radio 1 and 2 are broadcast on FM and can be widely received across Cyprus. BFBS Television is now only available to viewers via satellite, having been confined to the SBAs or encrypted in 1997 for copyright reasons, before BFBS switched off its analogue transmitters in 2009. The British East Mediterranean Relay Station was situated locally.

Frank D'Angelo, through his company In Your Ear Productions, has written, produced, directed, scored, and starred in seven feature-length films. His first film, *Real Gangsters* (2013), was followed by *The Big Fat Stone*, *No Deposit*, and the 2015 horror drama *Sicilian Vampire*. His 2016 release, *The Red Maple Leaf*, featured an ensemble cast including James Caan and Paul Sorvino. In 2018, D'Angelo ventured into dark comedy with *The Joke Thief*, co-starring Daniel Baldwin and Alyson Court. His films are distributed on platforms such as iTunes, Google Play, Amazon, and the In Your Ear Productions website. Known for his prolific output, D'Angelo's works have garnered attention on the film festival circuit, with *The Joke Thief* touring festivals in Toronto, New York, Boston, and Los Angeles. As of now, no films have been released after *The Joke Thief*.

Child composes music for orchestra, chorus, computer synthesis, voice, and chamber groups. His compositional style has been compared to Charles Ives, Benjamin Britten, and Gustav Mahler. Among his works are Embers (1984), a one-act chamber opera based on the play by Samuel Beckett, and Clare Cycle (1984), four settings from the poetry of John Clare.

Brian Graham attended the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA), where he played both baseball and football. He held multiple school records in baseball upon completing his collegiate career. In 1978, he was a member of the UCLA football team. Graham was inducted into the UCLA Baseball Hall of Fame in 1990. He later earned a bachelor's degree in behavioral sciences from California State University in 1985.

The Andalusian horse is descended from the Iberian horses of Spain and Portugal, and derives its name from its place of origin, the Spanish region of Andalusia. Cave paintings show that horses have been present on the Iberian Peninsula as far back as 20,000 to 30,000 BCE. Although Portuguese historian Ruy d'Andrade hypothesized that the ancient Sorraia breed was an ancestor of the Southern Iberian breeds, including the Andalusian, genetic studies using mitochondrial DNA show that the Sorraia is part of a genetic cluster that is largely separated from most Iberian breeds.

Kaiser married on 9 July 2010 former Borussia Dortmund football player Lars Ricken, with whom she was dating since early 2009. On 2 August 2013, it was announced that Kaiser and Ricken separated. Soon after, Kaiser announced her new relationship with French rally driver Sébastien Ogier. They married in summer 2014 and became parents of a son named Tim on 13 June 2016.

Several U.S. airlines have announced plans to install picocells on aircraft to enable passengers to use mobile phones during flights. This technology, which requires approval from organizations such as the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) and the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), is designed to create a controlled and low-power network environment within the aircraft. Similar to existing methods used in Europe, the aircraft would carry a base station, known as a picocell, to establish a localized network. The picocell functions by routing phone traffic to a satellite, which then connects to mobile networks on the ground. By blocking signals from ground-based towers, the picocell ensures that phones onboard do not interfere with terrestrial networks. Additionally, because the picocell's antennas are located close to passengers and within the aircraft's metal shell, both the picocell and mobile phones can operate at very low power levels, reducing the risk of interference with the aircraft's systems. This setup not only enhances connectivity for passengers but also ensures compliance with safety standards. The introduction of this technology could mark a significant step toward modernizing in-flight communication, allowing passengers to make calls, send messages, and use internet services seamlessly while in the air.

After production of the series ceased, Warringah Council granted land use access for Waratah Park to be used as an "animal park and entertainment area" in December 1969. Ed Devereaux was at the official opening in January 1970, appearing in uniform as Matt Hammond. Initially operated by Fauna Productions, the management of the park changed hands over the years, being held by Waratah Park Pty Ltd from 1976 and by Waratah Park Earth Sanctuary from 1995 to 2000. Visitors could see kangaroos and other wildlife including koalas, dingoes, emus and wombats. Visitors could also see the home of Skippy by walking through the Ranger Headquarters and seeing the film set used for interior scenes, still in situ.

She was born in Columbia, South Carolina, the daughter of Isadora Bennett and Daniel Reed, respectively a theater publicist and actor. Folk music collector Carl Sandburg and musician Huddie Ledbetter were family friends, as were visiting Irish actors and musicians from the Abbey Theatre Company. After she moved to New York City with her family – including her older brother Jared, who also performed – she began singing and playing the zither, harp and autoharp at private parties and for wounded soldiers convalescing in hospitals. Although she originally aspired to becoming a painter, she was discovered by Barney Josephson, owner of the Café Society club in Greenwich Village, who booked her to appear there.

Until the 1940s, some of the schools in the district were racially segregated, until the desegregation of the previously all-black Brooks School in 1943. Thurgood Marshall who served as Associate Justice of the U.S. Supreme Court, litigated this case as head of the NAACP legal team. Marshall later litigated and won Brown v. Board of Education of Topeka (Kansas).

In December 2005, the 1st Regiment, Royal Canadian Horse Artillery (1 RCHA) conducted the inaugural firing of its first six 155 mm M777 towed howitzers, supplied by the United States Marine Corps under a Foreign Military Sales (FMS) contract between the U.S. and Canada. The guns were first fired by "A" Battery, 1 RCHA, at CFB Shilo before being deployed to Afghanistan in support of Operation Archer. In early 2006, the M777 howitzers were put into service in the Kandahar region, providing critical artillery support during intense combat operations.

At the Paralympics, participants are grouped into five main disability categories: amputee, cerebral palsy, intellectual disability, visually impaired, and Les Autres (French for "the others"). The amputation category includes athletes missing at least one major joint, such as an arm amputated at the elbow or a leg at the knee, with some competing as wheelchair athletes. The cerebral palsy category encompasses athletes with movement, balance, and muscle control impairments due to brain damage. Visual impairments range from correctable conditions to total blindness. The Les Autres category includes athletes with locomotive disorders, such as dwarfism, that do not fit other classifications. Events are coded by sport-specific classifications to ensure fair competition. For example, athletics groups athletes based on physical demands, while swimming uses numerical categories (1–10 for physical impairments, 11–14 for vision or intellectual impairments) to reflect the severity of impairments. This system ensures athletes compete with minimal impact from their disabilities.

Ta'Niya Latson began her high school basketball career at Westlake High School in Atlanta, Georgia, where she played as a freshman alongside notable teammate Raven Johnson. During her time at Westlake, Latson helped lead the team to three consecutive state championships, including the State 7A Title in her sophomore year, and a GEICO National title in her junior year, where she scored 31 points in the final. For her senior season, she transferred to American Heritage School in Plantation, Florida, where she led the team to the Class 5A state title while averaging 27.2 points, 8.0 rebounds, 7.0 assists, 5.3 steals, and 2.0 blocks per game. A McDonald's All-American and Naismith nominee, Latson was rated as the top shooting guard in ESPN's 2022 rankings. She committed to Florida State University, choosing the Seminoles over offers from Baylor, Georgia, Kentucky, Miami, NC State, Texas, and Virginia Tech.

Fitchville Township, Huron County, Ohio, is governed by a three-member board of trustees. Trustees are elected to four-year terms, with two members elected in the year following a presidential election and one member elected in the year following a gubernatorial election. Terms begin on January 1 following the election. Additionally, an elected township fiscal officer oversees financial matters, serving a four-year term that begins on April 1 following their election, which occurs in the year before a presidential election. These officials ensure the effective administration of township affairs.

The Owatonna Firemen's Hall is also the headquarters for Steele County Skywarn, a non-profit weather spotting organization. Locally, it is a part of Steele County Emergency Management. It uses the third floor for meetings and another room in the building as a nerve center, to communicate with spotters out in the field, monitor weather conditions in and around Owatonna, and make reports to the National Weather Service in Chanhassen, Minnesota.

In October 1957, a Lt. Coughlin of the Kansas City Police Department's (KCPD) vice squad was investigating the distribution of magazines which might have met the state's definition of obscenity. As part of that investigation, he visited the office of Kansas City News Distributors, a wholesaler which sold all types of printed material to newsstands all over the city and its metropolitan area. He showed Homer Smay, the manager, a list of a possibly obscene magazine titles and asked if he distributed any of them; Smay confirmed that the wholesaler distributed all but one.

In China, disability pensions are governed by central government guidelines and categorized into 10 degrees of disability. Basic pension insurance provides 40% of the insured's monthly wage. For a first-degree disability, the benefit includes a lump sum of 27 months of the insured's previous wage and a monthly pension of 90% of their wage, while a sixth-degree disability provides a lump sum of 16 months' wages or a monthly benefit of 60% if the employer cannot offer suitable work. A tenth-degree disability grants a lump sum of seven months' wages. Temporary disability benefits are calculated as a percentage of the insured's wage, depending on the degree of disability, and are paid until maximum medical improvement is reached. The minimum benefit amount is determined by provincial and local governments based on the local standard of living.

The magazine *Studime Filologjike* originated as *Buletini i Institutit të Shkencave* (Bulletin of the Institute of Sciences), published by the Albanian Institute of Sciences, established in 1947. The Albanian Institute of History, a key institution in post-World War II Albania, was founded in 1946, playing a significant role in advancing academic research and national identity during the early years of Albania's independence. The original magazine featured sections on history, linguistics, and cultural studies. After its first issue, the magazine was renamed *Buletini i Institutit të Shkencave* and was published under this name by the Institute of Sciences from 1948 to 1957. In 1957, the magazine split into two separate publications: *Studime Historike* (Historical Studies) and *Studime Filologjike* (Philological Studies). The Bulletin for Social Studies focused on history and social sciences, with article resumes provided in Albanian and other languages to reach a broader audience.

Hungary qualified five boxers for the Olympic boxing tournament. Szellő qualified at the World Championships. Varga and Kalucza brought the Hungarian boxing squad up to four by qualifying at the first European qualifying event. Bedák was the fifth and final Hungarian to join the team, at the 2nd European tournament.

Alan is a masculine given name in the English language. The name is believed by scholars to have been brought to England by people from Brittany, in the 11th century; later the name spread north into Scotland and west into Ireland. In Ireland and Scotland there are Gaelic forms of the name which may, or may not, be etymologically related to the name introduced by the Bretons.

Sullivan was educated at the Carleton University School of Journalism, and worked as a journalist and later as a public affairs consultant. She lived in Hamilton, Ontario. Prior to her election to the Ontario Legislature, she was Principal Secretary to the Treasurer (Minister of Finance) in Ontario, serving both as chief of staff and lead policy advisor to the Minister. She was past director and vice-chair of Bird Studies Canada, chaired the Board of Directors of the Oakville Centre for the Performing Arts, patron of Sheridan College's Performing Arts program, and Commissioner and Acting Chair of the Commission on Election Finances in Ontario.
