KOKI-DT2, the second digital subchannel of KOKI-TV, broadcasts in standard definition on virtual channel 23.2. It originally served as an affiliate of TheCoolTV, launched as part of a groupwide agreement between Newport Television and Cool Music Network, LLC, covering 11 markets. KOKI-TV introduced the subchannel in 2010. On September 19, 2011, KOKI-DT2 became an affiliate of MeTV, a network specializing in classic television programming, following the termination of Newport's agreement with TheCoolTV.

Werner & Mertz demonstrated its commitment to environmental responsibility by introducing the first phosphate-free household cleaning agent under the Frosch brand in 1986. The company has since expanded its portfolio with eco-conscious brands like Erdal, tana PROFESSIONAL, green care PROFESSIONAL, Rorax, Emsal, and Bionicdry, adopting a strong market position in corporate social responsibility. Since 2009, emsal products have incorporated plant-based ingredients, while the Erdal shoe care range has been solvent-free. These efforts reflect Werner & Mertz's dedication to sustainability and innovation in hygiene and cleaning solutions.

As of the 2011 Census of India, Karaikal has 21,320 households with a total of 86,838 people. Of these 48.7% are male and 51.3% are female. There are 9,516 children in the age group 0-6. Scheduled Castes comprised about 11.3% of the population. The literacy rate was 79%.

June James was born on October 11, 1990, in Houston, Texas where he grew up. James studied at Texas Southern University where he was a member of the Ocean of Soul alongside The Chopstars DJ Hollygrove. While a member he met other artists and DJs which led him to curate LAD DJ's – a coalition started in 2011 that grew to about 40 members. He also founded the music group, The Hit Cartel in 2014 which includes producers, engineers, songwriters, disc jockeys, and artists.

In 2021, Bella Taylor Smith auditioned for season 10 of *The Voice Australia* with Beyoncé's "Ave Maria," earning four chair turns. She joined Team Guy Sebastian and was crowned the winner during the pre-recorded grand final, broadcast on September 12, 2021.

On June 15, 1926, the Red Sox traded Ehmke to the Philadelphia Athletics in exchange for Fred Heimach, Slim Harriss, and Baby Doll Jacobson. The change of scene did wonders for Ehmke. After going 3–10 with a 5.46 ERA in the first half of 1926 with the last place Red Sox, Ehmke went 12–4 with a 2.81 ERA in the second half of the season with Hall of Famer Mickey Cochrane catching and A's slugger, Al Simmons, hitting .341 behind him. Though the frequency of his starts diminished after 1927, Ehmke had a winning record for the Athletics in four consecutive seasons from 1926 to 1929.

Twenty-three trainees are brought to a complex (called the I-Land) in the middle of nowhere surrounded by a luscious green forest for 113 days. The trainees will live together in the three-floor complex with modern living spaces, gym, dance studios, item room (for storage), and individual practice rooms; however, only 12 contestants can stay at any one time. The others will be on the "Ground", a simple building with a dance studio and dining area, and will have to commute to and from the I-Land building every day at set times. The two living spaces are connected by a gate and stage, where their skills will be evaluated. A key part of the show's concept is that both the participants' and producers' votes play a heavy role, as they decide who stays in I-Land and who goes to the Ground. Although the I-Land only accommodates 12 trainees, the trainees may be dropped and demoted to the Ground and promptly be replaced by trainees from the Ground.

In the comic book *Charmed: Season 9*, Paige Matthews struggles with guilt over her inability to prevent the massacre of the magical community orchestrated by Christy Jenkins and the Triad. The series also explores the lingering impact of Christy’s betrayal, with Billie Jenkins shown as still coping with the emotional aftermath in both the comic books and novels based on the *Charmed* television series. In issue 16 of *Charmed: Season 9*, Prue Halliwell reveals that Billie and Christy were never destined to be powerful enough to defeat the Charmed Ones, a fact that underscores the manipulation they endured. Prue also explains that her bond to the Charmed Ones' prophecy, even after her death, restricted her sisters from achieving their full potential, leaving them vulnerable to the Triad’s schemes. Additionally, Leo Wyatt keeps a charred piece of wood from the Halliwell Manor as a reminder to Piper that their home is no longer a "danger-free zone." Christy does not make a physical appearance in the comics or novels.

As of the census of 2010, there were 795 people, 277 households, and 187 families living in the village. The population density was 993.8 inhabitants per square mile (383.7/km2). There were 298 housing units at an average density of 372.5 per square mile (143.8/km2). The racial makeup of the village was 98.5% White, 0.6% African American, 0.1% Asian, and 0.8% from two or more races. Hispanic or Latino of any race were 0.3% of the population. 65.1% were of German, 14.3% Irish, 9.5% Swiss, and 5.8% English descent.

The first Broadway revival of *Funny Girl* began previews at the August Wilson Theatre on March 26, 2022, and officially opened on April 24, 2022. Directed by Michael Mayer, the production featured a revised book by Harvey Fierstein, adapting Isobel Lennart's original. Beanie Feldstein starred as Fanny Brice, with Ramin Karimloo as Nick Arnstein, Jane Lynch as Mrs. Brice, and Jared Grimes as Eddie Ryan, the latter earning a Tony Award nomination for Best Featured Actor in a Musical. On July 10, 2022, Feldstein announced her early departure from the production, citing the creative team's decision to take the show in a different direction. Lea Michele was subsequently announced as her replacement, beginning performances on September 6, 2022. Tovah Feldshuh was also announced to replace Jane Lynch as Mrs. Brice on the same date. Lynch, originally scheduled to depart on September 25, moved her final performance to August 14, with Liz McCartney stepping in as Mrs. Brice until Feldshuh's arrival. Julie Benko, the production's standby, played Fanny Brice in the interim between Feldstein's departure on July 31 and Michele's debut.

The La Salle Explorers women's basketball team, as of the 2015–16 season, held an overall record of 3.37 GPA-academic excellence. They joined the Atlantic 10 Conference in 1992, leaving the Metro Atlantic Athletic Conference (MAAC), where they were regular season champions in 1987. Despite this, they missed the NCAA Tournament that year. The Explorers played in the MCC Tournament in 1992 and won their only conference championship tournament in 1987 under Farrell's leadership.

It was reported in the Argus 21 October 1852- "Bushrangers at Aitken's Gap-Five armed and mounted bushrangers "bailed" up and robbed a gentleman riding in the neighbourhood of Aitken's Gap on Tuesday morning last about 11 o'clock".  A settlement also grew at Aitken’s Gap, which was one of the key stages of the trip to the gold-fields. It was still a wild place in 1858 when traveller William Kelly arrived on the coach:- "Arrived at the top, it was a scene of extraordinary bustle and uproar, for it was then a special camping place for drivers and carriers, and the scores upon scores of horse drays and bullock-wagons that were preparing for a start, produced an amount of tumult, altercation, blasphemy, and compound abominations which would not find many readers even if I succeeded in reproducing it."

In the early 1960s, Alan Douglas assisted Duke Ellington with errands in Paris while Ellington worked on the score for *Paris Blues*. After his time in Paris, Douglas joined United Artists and moved to New York, where Ellington made a surprise visit, suggesting they record a piano-focused album. Douglas proposed Charles Mingus as the bassist, while Mingus insisted on Max Roach as the drummer. Mingus had briefly deputized for Ellington’s regular bassist in 1953 but was fired after an on-stage altercation over a racial slur. This collaboration ultimately led to the creation of *Money Jungle*, a landmark jazz album featuring Ellington, Mingus, and Roach.

The C40 Recharge, derived from the XC40 Recharge released on 2 March 2021, began production in September 2021. It is exclusively available as a battery electric vehicle and features a coupe-style roofline.

Set in November 1830, *Captain Swing* follows the tragic figure of Matthew Dinely, a struggling farm labourer and community leader, as he grapples with the rising unrest among impoverished workers in a Sussex village. Dinely, committed to non-violent action, attempts to control the growing anger of the mob protesting against exploitative landowners and the dire working conditions of the rural poor. However, his efforts are undermined by the enigmatic and incendiary figure of Captain Swing, whose threatening letters to landowners incite further chaos. As tensions escalate, Dinely faces a moral dilemma: whether to maintain his principles or succumb to the mob’s demands for more radical action. His adversary is not only the oppressive landowning class but also the volatile spirit of rebellion embodied by Captain Swing, whose true identity and motives remain shrouded in mystery, fueling the play’s exploration of justice and desperation.

In Finland, cabinet collective responsibility is both a constitutional principle and a convention, with the entire government collectively responsible for all decisions. Ministers may express dissent, but if they do, they are expected to resign. Following President Kekkonen’s retirement in 1981, coalition governments became the norm, reflecting Finland’s proportional representation system. A new cabinet must be approved by the Eduskunta (parliament) and is bound by a government platform agreed upon by participating parties. If a party fails to adhere to this platform, it may be forced to leave the coalition. The Finnish constitution emphasizes collective responsibility, with the prime minister and ministers working together to ensure unified governance, while maintaining respect for the rule of law and democratic principles.

Aubertin Walter Sothern Mallaby was born on December 12, 1899, in England to William Calthorpe Mallaby, an actor known by his stage name William Calthorpe Deeley, and Katherine Frances Mary (née Miller). His maternal grandfather was Aubertin Miller. Educated at Wellington College in India, Mallaby later joined the 27th Punjab Regiment in 1918. His younger brother, George Mallaby, held a prominent position as First Civil Service Commissioner from 1957 to 1959.

KOKI-DT2 is the MeTV-affiliated second digital subchannel of KOKI-TV, broadcasting in standard definition on channel 23.2. KOKI-TV launched a digital subchannel on virtual channel 23.2 in April 2011, originally serving as an affiliate of music video-focused network TheCoolTV through a groupwide agreement between network parent Cool Music Network, LLC and Newport Television, involving Newport-operated stations in ten markets. On July 30, 2012, through an affiliation agreement reached between Newport Television and network owner Weigel Broadcasting, KOKI-DT2 became an affiliate of the classic television network MeTV.

A month before the publication of *Leaving a Doll’s House*, Dinitia Smith reported for *The New York Times*, commenting on the intense gossip surrounding the memoir, which painted Philip Roth as a "self-centered misogynist." Smith noted that Claire Bloom was unable to comment on the book prior to its release, while Roth and his representatives also declined to respond. Patricia Bosworth reviewed the memoir for *The New York Times* shortly after its publication, questioning why Bloom endured Roth’s alleged duplicity and did not leave the relationship sooner. Bosworth described Bloom’s portrayal of Roth as scathing and bitter, highlighting the memoir’s one-sided narrative. Writing for the *London Review of Books*, Zoë Heller suggested that Bloom’s account revealed a complex dynamic, implying that Bloom may have remained emotionally tied to Roth despite the relationship’s toxicity, reflecting a broader theme of need versus desire in intimate partnerships.

After finishing at the bottom of the County Championship from 1952 to 1955, Somerset adopted a wide-ranging recruitment policy, which led to the signing of Lewis Pickles, a fair-haired right-handed opening batsman from Wakefield, Yorkshire. Pickles had previously played for Yorkshire's Second XI and was stationed in Somerset during his national service in 1953, where he came to the county club's attention. He played a couple of games for Somerset in 1955 before becoming the regular opener in the 1956 season. That year, he scored 1,136 runs at an average of 24, earning his county cap. His highest score of 87 came against Lancashire at Old Trafford, facing bowlers Brian Statham, Roy Tattersall, and Malcolm Hilton. Wisden noted that Pickles and fellow Yorkshireman Malcolm Walker showed promise as a regular opening partnership, though Walker's loss of form prevented its development.

The church is thought to have been built in the 12th or 13th century. It was used regularly until 1850, subsequently used only for a few services during the summer months. As recently as the early 20th century, coracles were used by worshippers to reach the church in its location beside the River Loughor. It went out of use in 1970 and began to be demolished in 1984 in readiness for the move to St Fagans.

The NIT2 gene in humans has been found to be identical to omega-amidase. The gene has the highest expression in the liver and kidney, but is also expressed in almost every human tissue. Overexpression of the NIT2 gene results in decreasing cell proliferation and growth in HeLa cells, which indicates that the gene may have a role in tumor suppression. However further studies are necessary to determine the effect on specific cancers, as a study done with colon cancer cells showed that downregulation of NIT2 induced cell cycle arrest. In addition to tumor suppression, NIT2/omega-amidase may be useful for detection and conversion of oncometabolites. Because omega-amidase is able to control concentration of toxic substrates such as KGM, it is likely that NIT2 can serve the same purpose.

Spurling's test is somewhat specific when used for individuals with an abnormal electromyogram study and is a relatively sensitive physical examination maneuver in diagnosing cervical spondylosis or acute cervical radiculopathy. It is not a very sensitive test when used for individuals without classic radicular signs suggestive of cervical radiculopathy. In 2011, one study evaluated 257 patients with clinical cervical radiculopathy and correlated CT scan findings with clinical exam findings using the Spurling's test. The Spurling's test was 95% sensitive and 94% specific for diagnosing nerve root pathology.

John C. Mankins had a distinguished 25-year career at NASA, during which he served for 10 years as the manager of the Office of Advanced Concepts and Technology at NASA Headquarters. In this role, he led critical studies on space solar power, highly reusable space transportation, and affordable human exploration approaches. Mankins was the conceptual mind behind several groundbreaking projects, including the "MagLifter" electromagnetic launch assist system, the "Solar Clipper" interplanetary transport vehicle, the "SunTower" space solar power system, and the "Integrated Vehicle Health Management" in-space refueling module. He also co-created the concept of the "Virtual Research Center," an Internet-based platform for collaborative research. Following the dissolution of the Advanced Concepts office by NASA Administrator Daniel Goldin, Mankins transitioned to managing the Exploration Systems Research and Technology program within the Exploration Systems Mission Directorate, overseeing a $800 million annual budget and more than 100 projects. His innovative contributions have left a lasting impact on space exploration and technology development.

The Johan Cruyff Arena, formerly Amsterdam Arena, was home to the Amsterdam Admirals until the NFL ended its European competition in 2007. From 1997 to 2007, the Admirals played numerous matches there. In 2001, the stadium hosted World Bowl IX, where the Berlin Thunder defeated the Barcelona Dragons 24-17 to claim their first title.

Corbelled houses in Williston, Fraserburg, and Carnarvon were built by early trekboer farmers between the early 1800s and late 19th century. Regarded as architectural treasures of the northwest Karoo, these structures feature domed roofs made of flat dolerite and sandstone, as wood was scarce. The term 'corbelled' refers to the overlapping stone construction technique. Floors were made of smeared earth, mixed with fat and oxblood for a rich red color, and polished with smooth stones, showcasing the settlers' resourcefulness.

Jack Hamilton, who came through the youth ranks at Heart of Midlothian after joining from Stenhousemuir in 2009, was promoted to the first team in September 2011 to provide cover due to an injury crisis. He was named as a substitute for a match against Inverness Caledonian Thistle but did not make an appearance. On November 30, 2012, he joined Forfar Athletic on loan until January 6, 2013, and later signed for East Stirlingshire on loan in March 2014.

The 2016 United States presidential election followed the constitutional requirements outlined in Article Two of the U.S. Constitution, which state that the President and Vice President must be natural-born citizens, at least 35 years old, and residents of the United States for at least 14 years. Candidates for the presidency typically seek the nomination of a political party, which is formally decided at national nominating conventions. These conventions are informed by the results of primary elections and caucuses, where voters cast ballots to determine their preferred party candidate. Primary elections, considered more inclusive and democratic, allow registered voters to participate in a secret ballot process. In the general election, held in November, voters across the country cast ballots for their preferred presidential and vice-presidential candidates. The general election ultimately determines the President and Vice President, making it the final step in the electoral process.

Conceived in Havana, Cuba and born in New York City, Walter Salas-Humara grew up in South Florida with his two older brothers after his family relocated to Ft. Lauderdale when he was three. He began playing drums at age seven, performing with a series of prog-rock and disco bands throughout adolescence. A cross-country road trip before his senior year of high school, led directly to his taking up the guitar and beginning to write songs. He attended the University of Florida in Gainesville, where he studied visual arts and received a Bachelor of Fine Arts degree. While in college, he co-founded the original version of the Vulgar Boatmen.

In October 1957, Lieutenant Coughlin of the Kansas City Police Department's vice squad conducted an investigation into the distribution of allegedly obscene magazines. As part of the investigation, he visited Kansas City News Distributors, a wholesaler of magazines, newspapers, and books, managed by Homer Smay. During the visit, Lt. Coughlin presented Smay with a list of magazine titles, and Smay admitted that his company distributed all but one of the listed titles. This investigation also included visits to five retail newsstands, where Lt. Coughlin purchased magazines as part of his inquiry.

Every two years, TELA commissions an independent research company to conduct a large-scale survey to collect the public's views on the film classification system and the film classification standards. In 2000, a survey was conducted on 617 members of the public, aged 13 to 59 years old. In addition, supplementary surveys were conducted on 108 members on the panel of advisers and 472 moviegoers. The survey findings were that almost all respondents were aware the classification system was in place, and 59% could identify the current system, as well as its symbols and their meanings.

At Dartmouth College, Sarah Devens participated on three varsity teams: field hockey, ice hockey, and lacrosse. She was named captain of all three teams, a rare achievement that underscored her exceptional athletic ability. Joan Hooley first appeared on television in the 1950s and was cast in the ITV hospital soap opera *Emergency – Ward 10*, appearing in the series in 1964. She played Dr Louise Mahler, a surgeon who became part of a groundbreaking and controversial storyline involving an interracial relationship with Dr Giles Farmer, portrayed by John White. The relationship included what was long thought to be the first interracial kiss on British television. However, a love scene between the two characters was rewritten as it was deemed "a little too suggestive" for the time. The storyline sparked significant media attention, with Hooley finding herself under headlines in the press. Following the controversy, her character, who had been prominent in the series, was quietly written out, marking a significant moment in television history and reflecting societal attitudes of the era.

Destruction to the olfactory tract results in ipsilateral anosmia (loss of the ability to smell). Anosmia either total or partial is a symptom of Kallmann syndrome a genetic disorder that results in disruption of the development of the olfactory tract. The depth of the olfactory sulcus is an indicator of such congenital anosmia.

Tadeusz Olszewski, born in Warsaw in 1921, graduated in architecture from the Warsaw Polytechnics after returning to communist Poland. He made his debut as a poet in 1947 with the publication of the poetry collection *Niepokój* (*Unrest*), which was highly praised by literary figures such as Leopold Staff and Czesław Miłosz. Between 1964 and 1981, Olszewski worked as the general designer of urban planning for cities including Warsaw, Baghdad, and Singapore, while also serving as a United Nations expert and lecturer. As a journalist, he contributed to *Ilustrowany Magazyn Turystyczny Światowid* during the 1960s and held the role of editor at *Tygodnik Kulturalny* from 1984 to 1986. From 1987 to 1990, he was the head of the poetry department at the monthly *Okolice*. In 1985, Olszewski publicly came out as gay and published an essay on homoerotic themes in modern Polish prose. He edited Poland’s first gay male monthly, *Etap*, in 1991–1992 under the pen name "Jan Nowicki" and published the poetry collection *Czerwona rękawiczka* (*Red Glove*) in 1992, a groundbreaking work addressing LGBT+ themes. From 1996 to 2004, he was associated with a major publishing house, contributing reviews and literary sketches to various publications. Olszewski, a member of several poetry competition juries, lived in Warsaw until his death.

Butcher was recruited as a vocalist for My Bloody Valentine in April 1987. She replaced original vocalist David Conway and shared vocal duties briefly with Joe Byfield. At her audition for the band, she sang "The Bargain Store", a song from Dolly Parton's 1975 album of the same name. The plot's transitions between past and present serve to reveal how past, historical actions inflict influence on the present day. Depicting descendants from major Native American historical figures additionally proves the dynamics between ancestral and current descendants, shaping past, present, and future actions. Past affecting present could additionally serve as a cause for Native American marginalization and issues within reservations. Native Americans in the United States are more than twice as likely to experience post-traumatic stress disorder when compared to the rest of the population. Native American demographics also have a life expectancy lowered by six years in comparison to the general U.S. population, as well as possess a doubled chance of living in poverty.

Celia's trial took place at a time when slavery was an extremely contentious issue in America, and the verdict had important implications for the legal status of enslaved persons, particularly black women. At that time, slaveowners were allowed to rape, when the legal status of slaves as property offered few protections or privileges of law. Legally, Celia was only seen as a human subject when she was being punished. As Saidiya Hartman states, "As Missouri v. Celia demonstrated, the enslaved could neither give nor refuse consent, nor offer reasonable resistance, yet they were criminally responsible and liable. The slave was recognized as a reasoning subject, who possessed intent and rationality, solely in the context of criminal liability."

These houses were originally built by pioneer farmers in Williston, Fraserburg and Carnarvon between 1811 and 1815. The Corbel House is regarded as the first architectural style in the northwest Karoo. Corbelled describes the manner in which stones are stacked in the construction of these buildings. The farmers used flat stones, which are common in the area, to build the homes. The floors were made of sand which had been mixed with animal fat and blood. Laws on drunkenness vary. In the United States, it is a criminal offense for a person to be drunk while driving a motorized vehicle, except in Wisconsin, where it is only a fine for the first offense. It is also a criminal offense to fly an aircraft or (in some American states) to assemble or operate an amusement park ride while drunk. Similar laws also exist in the United Kingdom and most other countries.

The Bañados del Izozog, a vast wetland located in the Bolivian department of Santa Cruz, receives most of its water from the Parapetí River, with additional contributions from smaller tributaries primarily originating in the Andes. Due to high year-round temperatures, much of the water evaporates. During wet years, the marshes overflow into the San Miguel River, which connects to the Madeira River and eventually the Amazon River. The Quimome River, a tributary of the Parapetí, flows into Lake Quimome. A significant portion of surface flow and infiltration from the Bañados del Izozog enters the Paraguay River catchment, part of the Río de la Plata Basin, moving southeastward.

CD38 was first identified in 1980 as a surface marker of thymic lymphocytes. By 1990, it was also described as a marker on B cells, monocytes, and natural killer cells, coinciding with the discovery of its enzymatic activity. CD38 was found to function as an NAD+ glycohydrolase, capable of synthesizing calcium-releasing second messengers, including cyclic adenosine diphosphate ribose (cADPR) and nicotinic acid adenine dinucleotide phosphate (NAADP). These messengers regulate intracellular calcium homeostasis, highlighting CD38's dual role as both a surface marker and a multifunctional enzyme critical to cellular signaling and metabolism.

Walter Salas-Humara, a Cuban-American musician, was conceived in Cuba and born in New York City after his parents fled Castro's Havana. Raised bilingual in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, with his two older brothers, his family relocated there when he was still an infant. Salas-Humara began playing drums at age seven, performing with prog-rock and disco bands during his adolescence. He later attended the University of Florida in Gainesville, where he studied visual arts and earned a degree in painting. While in college, he co-founded the Vulgar Boatmen, an experience that inspired him to take up the guitar and begin writing songs, shaping his future musical career.

BaaS providers generate revenue from their services in various ways, often using a freemium model. Under this model, a client receives a certain number of free active users or API calls per month, and pays a fee for each user or call over this limit. Alternatively, clients can pay a set fee for a package which allows for a greater number of calls or active users per month. There are also flat fee plans that make the pricing more predictable. Some of the providers offer the unlimited API calls inside their free plan offerings. While BaaS is most commonly offered as a commercial service, open-source options are available.

Hulme Grammar School traces its origins to Oldham Grammar School, founded in 1606 with land donated by Sir James Assheton of Chadderton. The school, originally a one-room building constructed in 1611, closed in 1867 due to industrial expansion making its location unsuitable. It was refounded in 1882 under the Endowed Schools Act of 1869, supported by funds from the Hulme Trust, established in 1691 by William Hulme. Elements of the original 1611 building, including a doorway and a window, were incorporated into the current school building during the 1920s. The refounded school marked a new era, with its first headmaster being Dr. Thomas C. Burton, who led the institution with a vision for modern education.

Horizontal gene transfer (HGT) can confound phylogenetic tree inference when based on a single gene, as the tree may reflect the gene's evolution rather than the organisms' evolutionary history. For instance, antibiotic resistance genes shared between distantly related bacteria can make them appear closely related. To ensure robust phylogenies, it is ideal to use combined data, such as concatenated protein-coding genes (PCGs) and ribosomal RNA (rRNA) genes, which increase informativeness and reduce errors. Including diverse genes accounts for evolutionary constraints and improves the accuracy and robustness of phylogenetic analyses.

Michael Korovkin has been described as a "brilliant and ferocious poet" by critics, with his last book of poetry to date, *Fields of Vision: Selected Poems and The Soldier’s Tale*, published in 2015. The collection includes 40 poems, with the opening piece titled "The Heart of a Dog," a nod to Bulgakov’s famous novella. A significant section of the book, *The Soldier’s Tale*, is a new rendering of the fable famously adapted by Stravinsky, subtitled "A Modern Parable of War and Loss." Korovkin’s work often explores themes of human arrogance, the apocalyptic, and the transformational power of art, reflecting his recurring focus on the intersections of history, absurdity, and the marginalization of intellectuals. These themes resonate throughout his poetry and prose, solidifying his reputation as a profound and critical voice in contemporary literature.

The core structural unit in many natural products, including azafluorenones, is the fluorenone framework, which has garnered significant research interest. Representative compounds like onychine, polyfothine, and isoursuline exhibit anti-malarial effects against *Plasmodium falciparum*. Cyathocaline interacts with DNA, while azafluorenone derivatives demonstrate antimalarial, antimicrobial, and anticancer activities. Additionally, they are used in fluorescence-based technologies and exhibit specific enzyme inhibition, highlighting their diverse biological and technological applications.

CNG auto rickshaws are very popular mode of transport for short routes within the city. The city's transport system links it to a number of destinations in Azad Kashmir notably Bhimber, Jatlan, Chakswari, Dadyal, Kotli and Khoi Ratta and to major cities in Pakistan as well as including services to Gujrat, Jhelum, Kharian, Gujranwala, Lahore and Rawalpindi. In March 1996, Blackstar, LLC, which included a nonvoting equity stake held by Fox, announced plans to affiliate KEVN-TV with the Fox network, ending its longtime affiliation with NBC. This move left Rapid City without an NBC affiliate. In response, Rapid Broadcasting, led by president Gilbert Moyle, acquired the low-power Christian television station K24AM, which had been broadcasting since the mid-1980s. Rapid Broadcasting increased the station's transmitter power and secured the NBC affiliation for the Rapid City area. The newly rebranded KNBN-LP officially launched on July 15, 1996, coinciding with KEVN-TV's switch to Fox. KNBN-LP immediately became available on local cable systems, ensuring NBC programming remained accessible to the region.

On July 2, 2013, Scott Feldman and Steve Clevenger were traded to the Baltimore Orioles from the Chicago Cubs in exchange for Jake Arrieta, Pedro Strop, and cash. Feldman reunited with his first big league manager, Buck Showalter, in Baltimore. He made his Orioles debut on the same day, starting against the Chicago White Sox and earning a win in a 4-2 victory. Feldman’s best start as an Oriole came on August 18, 2013, when he pitched a dominant game against the Colorado Rockies, leading the Orioles to a 7-2 win. On September 6, 2013, Feldman tossed the first complete game shutout of his career, allowing just five hits—all singles—against the Chicago White Sox, giving Baltimore its first complete game in over a year.

In Massachusetts, five-term incumbent and 2004 Democratic presidential nominee John Kerry, re-elected with 66% of the vote in 2008, resigned from the Senate in early 2013 to become Secretary of State. Governor Deval Patrick appointed Mo Cowan as interim Senator. Democratic Congressman Ed Markey won the June 25, 2013, special election to succeed Kerry, defeating Republican Gabriel Gomez by a 10-point margin. After completing Kerry's term, Markey sought a full term in 2014, facing Republican nominee Brian Herr in the general election.

Halloween and Halloween-like traditions, rooted in European customs, have spread globally, with countries such as Austria, Japan, and Singapore adopting unique celebrations. A distinctly American invention, the haunted attraction, began its global export in the late 20th century, with vendors supplying equipment and expertise to Europe and Asia. Theme parks have played a pivotal role in globalizing Halloween, with Universal Studios hosting Halloween Horror Nights in Florida, California, Japan, and Singapore since the 1990s. Disney has also expanded its Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween events to parks in Paris, Hong Kong, and Tokyo. Six Flags locations, such as Great Adventure in New Jersey, have embraced Halloween with Fright Fest celebrations. Overseas operators, inspired by the American trend, have developed their own haunted attractions, contributing to Halloween’s rise as a global phenomenon. This cultural export, alongside the influence of American horror films and theme parks, has transformed Halloween into a worldwide event, celebrated with festivals, parades, and scare-filled attractions.

In 1959, Lodge and Jacob married at the age of 24. Lodge later said, "It seems extraordinary now. I had no prospects, no job, little money but it never bothered me. We didn't really want children at the point they came along, but we got on with it." Equinox Minerals was formed through a merger with Equinox Resources in January 2004 and was incorporated as a Canadian company the same month. The company began its operations in Zambia in 1996, focusing on the Lumwana project, which spans two major copper mines—Malundwe and Chimiwungo—and covers an area of 1,355 square kilometers. Before Equinox acquired the project in 1999, at least 10 companies had failed to develop it. In 2010, Equinox acquired the Jabal Sayid copper project, located approximately 350 kilometers northeast of Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, with an annual ore production capacity of 60,000 tonnes starting in 2012. In February 2011, Equinox launched a $4.8 billion hostile takeover bid for Lundin Mining, which was simultaneously pursuing a $9 billion merger with Inmet Mining. In April 2011, Minmetals Resources Ltd. made a C$6.3 billion hostile takeover offer for Equinox, which was rejected. Shortly after, on April 25, 2011, Barrick Gold Corporation announced its acquisition of Equinox for C$7.2 billion, a deal that was accepted and marked Barrick’s second-largest acquisition after its 2005 purchase of Placer Dome. This acquisition granted Barrick access to Equinox’s key assets, including the Lumwana mine in Zambia and the Jabal Sayid project in Saudi Arabia.

The C40 Recharge is a derivative of the XC40 Recharge, which was released on 2 March 2021. It officially commenced production in September 2021. It is also the first Volvo nameplate that is only available as a battery electric vehicle.

Louis has written a large number of books and articles about the British Empire from 1940 to 1967, particularly concerning the Middle East,: 5 the Cold War, and the rise of American imperialism in the region. His first major book, Imperialism at Bay, 1941–1945 (1977), covers the contest between British and American officials over the fate of Britain's empire in the postwar world. His second and most famous book, The British Empire in the Middle East, 1945–1951 (1984), traces the critical years of Clement Attlee's Labour cabinet when the British government maintained their informal influence in the Middle East with the backing of the United States, which saw the British Empire as a bulwark against the spread of communism.

Owen Thomas Jones joined the British Geological Survey in 1910, working in the Midlands. In 1919, he was appointed the first professor of geology at Aberystwyth. He became professor of geology at the University of Manchester in 1920. Starting in 1930, Jones held the position of Woodwardian Professor of Geology at the University of Cambridge, a role he served with distinction.

Topock was originally called Mellen, a railroad station and steamboat landing, at the site where the Atlantic and Pacific Railroad built the Red Rock Bridge, a steel cantilever bridge across the Colorado River in May 1890 after three of its earlier less well-built bridges had been washed away by the river upstream at a poorly chosen site at Eastbridge, southeast of Needles, California. The town was named for Captain "Jack" Mellon, a 40-year veteran Colorado River steamboat captain and an owner of the Colorado Steam Navigation Company, though it was misspelled as "Mellen". From 1903 to 1909 Mellen had its own post office.: 113

Breast cancer affects 1 in 8 women, and an estimated 27% of people live at least 5 years after being diagnosed with Stage IV cancer according to the National Cancer Institute. Mammography is the most commonly used method to screen for cancer, but there are three major drawbacks. The first is ionizing radiation. Since mammography uses low-energy x-rays to image the breast, the breast is exposed to ionizing radiation. Too much repeated exposure can elevate the risk of cancer down the road. The second drawback is inaccuracy. Mammography has low specificity and this can lead to false positives, which detect abnormalities that never progress to cause symptoms or death and also false negatives, especially in dense breast tissue, when it is especially difficult to detect tumors. 60 to 80 out of every 100 biopsies performed after mammography are actually negative for cancer. And lastly, pain is a major drawback to mammography. 23-95% experience discomfort, and pain is a significant inhibitor to re-attending screenings.

The Andalusian horse, descended from Iberian and Barb horses, originated in the Spanish region of Andalusia, from which it takes its name. Cave paintings dating back to 20,000–30,000 BCE show the presence of horses on the Iberian Peninsula, likely ancestors of modern Iberian breeds. Portuguese historian Ruy d'Andrade hypothesized that the ancient Sorraia breed influenced Southern Iberian breeds, including the Andalusian. However, mitochondrial DNA studies reveal that the Sorraia forms a largely separate genetic cluster from most Iberian breeds, challenging this hypothesis.

The play *Sovereignty* transitions between past and present to reveal the enduring impact of historical trauma on Native American communities. By depicting the interplay between ancestral struggles and the lives of current descendants, it highlights how past injustices perpetuate marginalization and systemic issues on reservations. Native Americans are 2.4 years behind the general U.S. population in life expectancy and are 26% more likely to live in poverty compared to 12% nationally. They also face disproportionately high rates of post-traumatic stress disorder, being significantly more affected than the general population. These dynamics underscore the intergenerational effects of historical oppression and the ongoing challenges faced by Native communities in the United States.

The Gastraea hypothesis was proposed by Ernst Haeckel, shortly after his work on the calcareous sponges. He proposed that this group of sponges is monophyletic with all eumetazoans, including the bilaterians. This suggests that the gastrulation and the gastrula stage are universal for eumetazoans. It has been perceived as problematic that gastrulation by invagination is by no means universal among eumetazoans. Only recently has an invagination been confirmed in a Calcarea sponge, albeit too early to form a remaining inner space (archenteron).

Backend as a Service (BaaS) providers typically generate revenue through subscription-based or pay-as-you-go models. Many adopt a freemium approach, offering free access to basic features, such as limited API calls or active users, with charges applied once limits are exceeded. Clients can either pay for additional usage or opt for predictable fee plans, such as tiered subscriptions. Some providers include features like analytics or basic storage in their free plans. While open-source BaaS options exist, most services are delivered as Software as a Service (SaaS), ensuring ease of use and scalability. This model enables businesses to integrate financial or backend services without significant infrastructure investment.

Manner maintains accounts on various social networking services and online video platforms, including YouTube, where their channel has garnered over three billion views and six million subscribers as of recent reports. In 2017, the company released over 300 skits, and by 2018, their videos had accumulated over 1.5 billion views. That year, Manner had over 3.5 million followers across platforms, with a significant portion of their audience—approximately 1.2 million—residing in Guangdong. Their audience also extends to regions such as Hong Kong, Macau, and Southeast Asia. In Mainland China, viewers primarily access Manner’s content through platforms like WeChat and Weibo. The audience spans a wide age range, from teenagers to individuals in their 40s. The Macau Post Daily described Manner as the "KOL platform in Macau with the widest audience." Additionally, their relatable videos are frequently shared on Asian diaspora Facebook groups like Subtle Asian Traits and Subtle Cantonese Traits.

Te Tai Tokerau's boundaries are similar to those of the pre-Mixed Member Proportional (MMP) Northern Maori electorate. Te Tai Tokerau was created ahead of the first MMP election in 1996. In the 2002 boundary redistribution, the size of the electorate shrank to make room for an increase in the number of Māori electorates from six to seven. The boundaries were not further altered in the 2007 or 2013/14 redistributions.

United States law also provides for the formation of special grand juries. While a regular grand jury primarily decides whether to bring charges, a special grand jury is called into existence to investigate whether organized crime is occurring in the community in which it sits. This could include, for instance, organized drug activity or organized corruption in government. As provided in 18 U.S.C. § 3331(a), the U.S. District Court in every judicial district having more than four million inhabitants must impanel a special grand jury when requested by a designated official of the Justice Department.

Building on evidence obtained during the Norte Valle and Venezuela investigations, the El Dorado Task Force, DEA and Klapper obtained an indictment and conviction of Maximiliano Bonilla, also known as Valenciano. Bonilla was one of the most violent traffickers operating in Colombia. The investigation revealed that Bonilla was using Panama and Venezuela as a trans-shipment route for container-loads of cocaine once the route through Venezuela had been shut down and selling massive quantities of cocaine to the violence Mexican organizations, Los Zetas. Valenciano became a fugitive, was arrested in Venezuela and eventually sent to the United States, where he awaits sentencing. U.S. v. Maximiliano Bonilla, et al.

William Hutcheon Hall (1797?–1878) was born to William Hall and Mary Hutcheon. He joined the Royal Navy on 24 October 1811 as a first-class volunteer aboard HMS *Warrior* under Captains George Byng and John Tremayne Rodd, serving in the Channel, North Sea, and Baltic. Promoted to midshipman in 1813, he later served under Captain Basil Hall on HMS *Lyra* during Lord Amherst's 1816 embassy to China. After returning to England in November 1817, Hall was appointed to HMS *Iphigenia* under Sir Robert Mends in the West Africa Squadron and promoted to master of HMS *Morgiana*. He served in the West Indies, Mediterranean, and home stations until 1836. Hall then studied steam engines in Glasgow and on Irish steamers before traveling to the United States, where he worked on steamboats on the Hudson and Delaware Rivers. This early experience with steam power would later influence his naval career.

Angelo Sala (1576–1637), the son of Bernhardino Sala, a spinner, likely began studying pharmacy in northern Italy, near Vicenza. After his family converted to Calvinism in Geneva, he moved to Dresden (1602), Sondrio and Ponte (1604), Nuremberg (1606), and Frauenfeld (1607). From 1607 to 1609, Sala served as a city physician in Winterthur before joining Protestant forces as a physician in 1610.

Spurling's test is used to evaluate cervical radiculopathy, particularly in individuals with abnormal electromyogram studies. It is relatively sensitive for diagnosing nerve root pathology in patients with clinical cervical radiculopathy but less sensitive in those without classic radicular signs. A 2011 study assessed 257 patients with cervical radiculopathy, correlating CT scan findings with Spurling's test results. The test demonstrated 95% sensitivity and 94% specificity for diagnosing nerve root pathology, highlighting its diagnostic utility when combined with other clinical assessments.

One of Pius VII's first acts was appointing the minor cleric Ercole Consalvi, who had performed so ably as secretary to the recent conclave, to the College of Cardinals and to the office of Cardinal Secretary of State. Consalvi immediately left for France, where he was able to negotiate the Concordat of 1801 with the First Consul Napoleon. While not effecting a return to the old Christian order, the treaty did provide certain civil guarantees to the Church, acknowledging "the Catholic, Apostolic, and Roman religion" as that of the "majority of French citizens".
